function: bloaty::RangeSink::AddRange(char const*, std::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> >, unsigned long, unsigned long, unsigned long, unsigned long)
what_input_feature: A structurally coherent ELF input whose program/section-header offset and size fields (vmaddr/vmsize at the dword-aligned header slots, e.g. bytes around 0x10-0x27) decode to a vm range that is NOT covered by translator_->vm_map, driving the !CoversRange(vmaddr, vmsize) TRUE side at bloaty.cc:1331.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry a valid ELF magic plus structured 4-byte header values (e.g. 00 00 00 10 / 00 00 00 10 at 0x20-0x27, 50 55 markers at 0x18-0x19) so AddRange is invoked with concrete vmaddr/vmsize that I2S has tuned to land outside the base map, hitting the THROW true side 26 times.
why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds fill the header region with padding (long runs of 0x20 spaces and 0x00) rather than the precise dword values that decode to an out-of-range vm range; they reach line 1331 only on the FALSE side (L1331 T=0 F=550000) and never satisfy the TRUE condition.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the CoversRange / range-comparison CMP operands and substitutes the parsed comparand back into the matching input header offsets, letting cmplog land precise vmaddr/vmsize values that naive's blind havoc byte mutation cannot synthesize.

function: bloaty::Bloaty::ScanAndRollupFile(std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, bloaty::Rollup*, std::vector<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> >, std::allocator<std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > > >*) const
what_input_feature: The leading bytes must form a Mach-O magic constant (ce fa ed fe / cf fa ed fe) followed by enough valid Mach-O load-command/section structure (including __ section-name bytes 5f 5f) that file->GetBuildId() returns a non-empty string, so the true side of if (!build_id.empty()) is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds begin with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe (or cf fa ed fe) and carry embedded 5f 5f (__) section-name bytes and load-command sizing fields; these parse into a Mach-O whose build_id (UUID) is recovered, populating build_id and exercising lines 1751-1757 only in W.
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d (WASM \0asm) or similar non-Mach-O magic; bloaty parses them as a format that yields an empty build_id, so GetBuildId() returns empty and line 1750 always takes the false side (T=0 in L).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the object-file format CMPs (magic and structural-field comparisons) and I2SRandReplace splices the exact required literals (Mach-O magic, section-name bytes) into the input at the offsets where the parser compares them; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct the multi-field magic/structure by blind havoc, so they never produce a parseable build_id.

source_line: if (magic == MH_MAGIC || magic == MH_MAGIC_64 || magic == FAT_CIGAM) {
what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes of the input must equal the Mach-O magic word cf fa ed fe at offset 0x00-0x03 so that the read magic compares equal to one of MH_MAGIC / MH_MAGIC_64 / FAT_CIGAM at macho.cc:618, taking the true branch into the MachOObjectFile constructor.
why_winner_satisfies: Every cmplog winner-resolving seed begins with the exact bytes cf fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03, which is the little-endian Mach-O magic; the per-offset byte sets show these four offsets are fully concentrated to that value across all 10 winner seeds.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds begin with 00 61 73 6d ('\0asm', the WebAssembly magic), so magic never matches any Mach-O constant and execution falls through to the nullptr return at line 623, exercising the false branch.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the logged CMP operands from the magic==MH_MAGIC comparison and splices the constant magic value into the input at the matching offset; naive has no CMP introspection and cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte magic, so it never crosses the gate.

what_input_feature: The first four bytes must equal the Mach-O magic number ce fa ed fe (little-endian 0xFEEDFACE). Bloaty's format dispatch matches this magic to enter the Mach-O object reader, which calls AddRange / FindContaining repeatedly; without the magic match the input is routed to a different (e.g. WASM) parser whose path drives FindContaining only on the false side.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with ce fa ed fe at offsets 0x00-0x03; the BYTE DIFF shows W concentrated on ce/fa/ed/fe (and a few ASCII M/Z/L/E/P/F) at those offsets, and the winners reach FindContaining 562 times and its callers far more, with the blocker true side taken (T=101/160).
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed begins with 00 61 73 6d (the WASM magic '.asm'), so the Mach-O reader is never entered; FindContaining is still invoked 1720 times on the L set but the blocker true side is taken 0 times (T=0 F=1720), i.e. the loser parser path never produces the empty-result condition the true branch encodes.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the format-magic comparison and log both operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the seed's leading bytes and substitutes the expected magic operand (ce fa ed fe) directly at offset 0. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must stumble onto the exact 4-byte magic by chance via havoc, which is why naive never resolves (0/10) and value_profile resolves only 1/10.

function: bloaty::RangeMap::AddRangeWithTranslation(unsigned long, unsigned long, std::__cxx11::basic_string<char, std::char_traits<char>, std::allocator<char> > const&, bloaty::RangeMap const&, bool, bloaty::RangeMap*)
what_input_feature: A well-formed enough object file whose parser produces an AddRangeWithTranslation call where the size argument equals the kUnknownSize sentinel, so the True branch at range_map.cc:260 is taken. The winning seeds carry the Mach-O 64-bit magic ce fa ed fe at offset 0x00-0x03 and a structured header body, whereas the path that sets size==kUnknownSize requires the parser to advance deep enough to emit an unknown-sized range.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner seed begins with the Mach-O magic ce fa ed fe and shares an identical stable header (offsets 0x00-0x20 fixed across all 10 winners). These inputs drive 22887 hits on the branch and 140 invocations of AddFileRangeForVMAddr, reaching the True side (W: T=70 F=200 at L260) and resolving in 10/10 trials.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds carry the WASM magic 00 61 73 6d (\0asm) and are short (17-59 bytes); they reach AddRangeWithTranslation only on the False side (L: T=0 F=366 at L260) and never produce a size==kUnknownSize range, blocking the True branch in 10/10 trials with hitcount 0.
mechanism_attribution: The two fuzzers differ by exactly I2SRandReplace: value_profile_cmplog adds the cmplog input-to-state stage on top of the same comparison feedback value_profile already has. When the parser compares the running size against the kUnknownSize sentinel (a fixed wide constant), the cmplog CMP buffer logs that constant and I2SRandReplace can splice it into the input bytes that flow to the size field, directly synthesizing the equality value_profile's distance feedback only nudges toward. This is I2S comparison-solving on a magic-constant equality gate.

what_input_feature: An ELF input (magic 7f 45 4c 46) whose header/structural fields parse far enough to populate translator_ and invoke RangeMap::CoversRange via RangeSink::AddRange (bloaty.cc:1331), supplying a vmaddr/vmsize whose first lookup iterator either reaches mappings_.end() or fails EntryContains, so the else-if at range_map.cc:315 takes its true side and returns false.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving cmplog seeds carry a clean ELF magic plus concentrated structural bytes (e.g. 7f ff-marked fields at 0x09-0x0c and the PU/0x50 0x55 pair at 0x18-0x19) that satisfy the parser CMP gates so the translator_ branch is entered and CoversRange is reached, where the W-only lines 316-317 (return false) are hit and the branch true side covered.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking naive seeds carry malformed/space-filled bytes after the magic (runs of 0x20 across 0x0a-0x1c, swapped ELF class/data at 0x04-0x06) that never drive the parser through the translator_-establishing path with an uncovered address; their CoversRange invocations stay on the false side (line 315 L T=0 F=1260000) and the branch true side is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads intercepted integer/string CMP operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected operand into matching input offsets, letting it reconstruct the exact ELF header field values that the bloaty parser compares against; naive must hit those multi-byte field values by blind havoc and never does within budget, so only cmplog drives the input through the translator map construction that exercises the CoversRange true side.

what_input_feature: Bytes 0x00-0x03 must equal the literal magic 7f 45 4c 46 (\177ELF) so the memcmp guard at line 452 passes, AND byte 0x05 (ident[EI_DATA]) must hold a value other than ELFDATA2LSB(1) or ELFDATA2MSB(2) so the switch at line 468 falls through to the default arm at line 475.
why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog seeds keep the magic 7f 45 4c 46 at offsets 0x00-0x03 and place a non-{1,2} value at offset 0x05 (e.g. seed 1 has 0x30, seed 2 has 0x00, seed 3 has 0xe7), so EI_DATA misses both case labels and hits default.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds also keep the magic but set offset 0x05 to either 0x01 or 0x02 in every sample, so EI_DATA always matches case ELFDATA2LSB or ELFDATA2MSB and the switch never reaches default.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLog buffer for the switch comparison on ident[EI_DATA] and substitutes the recorded compared operand at the input offset, but unlike a single magic-number gate the winning move is to make the byte NOT equal the case labels; I2S surfaces the magic prefix (7f 45 4c 46) keeping line 452 satisfied while the switch on a single byte is escaped via a non-matching value, a combination cmplog reaches and naive's blind havoc rarely lands while preserving the magic.

what_input_feature: The 2-byte little-endian e_machine field at input offset 0x12-0x13 must hold 0x003e (62 = EM_X86_64). The switch on e_machine at elf.cc:782 dispatches the true arm only for this exact 16-bit value.
why_winner_satisfies: Winning cmplog seed 2a25258f656af261 carries 3e 00 at offset 0x12-0x13 (little-endian 0x003e = EM_X86_64), and seed 80ff9a7dac09a5e8 carries 3e 00 at offset 0x13-0x14; both supply the exact comparand the switch tests for.
why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds carry 00 at offset 0x12, never the 0x3e byte that the switch arm requires, so they fall through to the default arm and return false.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the switch comparison via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp callbacks, logs the constant 0x3e operand into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that exact byte into the input at the e_machine offset; naive has no CMP introspection and must hit 0x003e by blind havoc mutation, which it almost never does.

what_input_feature: The ELF header e_machine half-word must hold the exact 16-bit value EM_AARCH64 = 0x00B7 (low byte 0xB7, high byte 0x00) so that `switch (e_machine)` dispatches to `case EM_AARCH64:` at elf.cc:798. In the winner this lands at input bytes 0x12=0xB7, 0x13=0x00.
why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seed carries the bytes 01 d5 b7 00 at offsets 0x10-0x13, placing 0xB7 at offset 0x12; the per-role overlay marks line 798 case EM_AARCH64 as [W] (winner-only) and the divergent-branch table shows W takes the true side (T=3 F=0) while L never does (T=0 F=29).
why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser seed misses the EM_AARCH64 constant: the byte-diff shows offset 0x12 is 0xB7 only in W while L holds 00x8/03/01, so the switch falls through to `default:` (line 822, marked [L]) which returns false; the divergent-branch table shows L takes default true 26 times and EM_AARCH64 never.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's CMP-interception logs the `e_machine == EM_AARCH64` integer comparison operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the observed operand and substitutes the magic 0xB7 directly at the matching offset, solving the exact-equality roadblock in one step. naive has only havoc/token mutators with no CMP introspection, so it must blind-guess the 2-byte magic and never lands it across 10 trials.

function: elf.cc:bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ReadELFSymbols(bloaty::InputFile const&, bloaty::RangeSink*, std::map<std::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> >, std::pair<unsigned long, unsigned long>, std::less<std::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> > >, std::allocator<std::pair<std::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> > const, std::pair<unsigned long, unsigned long> > > >*, bool)::$_0::operator()(bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ElfFile const&, std::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> >, unsigned long) const
what_input_feature: The input must parse as an ELF whose header fields (magic 7f 45 4c 46, class/endianness/version bytes at 0x04-0x07, and the section-header-count field) decode to a section_count() value of 2 or more, so the loop guard i < elf.section_count() is true on the first iteration (i=1).
why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seeds carry well-formed ELF identification bytes (e.g. 7f 45 4c 46 02 02 03 00 ... and 7f 45 4c 46 02 01 ...) plus header fields that make section_count() large: in the W role section_count() is invoked 286000 times and the loop guard at line 856 takes the true side (T=57200), whereas the loser does not, so the loop body and ReadSection run only for the winner.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds keep section_count() effectively at <=1 so the line-856 guard never takes the true branch (L role at line 856 shows T=0 F=29); section_count() fires only 222 times in L vs 286000 in W, and ReadSection (line 536) fires 0 times in L, meaning the loop body is never entered.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand into input offsets matching the other operand, letting it plant the exact header byte values (magic/class/section-count) that the ELF parser compares against, so the decoded section_count() crosses the loop bound. Naive has only blind havoc with no CMP introspection and cannot reliably hit the multi-byte header constraints.

what_input_feature: An input that drives the vmsize argument of IsVerboseForVMRange to exactly RangeMap::kUnknownSize (the all-ones UINT64_MAX sentinel), i.e. a parsed 8-byte size field set to 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF rather than any concrete range length.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 10/10 trials, taking the true side (W: T=70 F=0 at L1103) and reaching the sentinel-only code at L1104-1105; its seeds carry concentrated 0xff bytes (e.g. offset 0x34 ff x6) inside the parsed header region, exactly the byte value the kUnknownSize equality wants substituted.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile blocks the branch in 10/10 trials, always taking the false side (L: T=0 F=2170 at L1103) and never executing the sentinel rewrite at L1104; lacking I2SRandReplace it cannot place the exact 8-byte UINT64_MAX sentinel into the size field, so the equality never holds.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace to value_profile's stack; I2S logs the runtime CMP operand kUnknownSize (UINT64_MAX) and substitutes it directly into the matching input bytes, satisfying the exact-equality gate. value_profile's CMP_MAP only buckets operand distance and cannot synthesize the all-ones constant, so it stalls at the false side.

function: elf.cc:bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ReadELFTables(bloaty::InputFile const&, bloaty::RangeSink*)::$_0::operator()(bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ElfFile const&, std::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> >, unsigned int) const
what_input_feature: A valid ELF header at offset 0x00 (magic 7f 45 4c 46 plus class/data/version fields) whose section-table fields (e_shnum / e_shoff and related header words at offsets ~0x04-0x2f) parse such that elf.section_count() returns a value >= 2, making the loop condition i < section_count() true for i starting at 1.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the ELF magic and structured header bytes with non-0x20 values in the class/data/version and section-count region (e.g. 02 02 03 at offsets 0x04-0x06, concentrated low integer values at 0x07-0x2f), so the parsed header yields section_count() of 286000 entry hits in W and ReadSection fires; the true side of branch 991 is taken (W resolves 10/10).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds fill the section-table header region with 0x20 (space) padding runs (offsets 0x0a-0x1c are dominated by 20 20 20 ... in L), so the parsed header gives section_count()==0/1; section_count() entry-line is hit only 222 times in L vs 286000 in W and the loop body (ReadSection, line 993) is never entered, so the false side is taken (L blocks 10/10).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads CMP operands from the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer (here the integer comparison i < section_count() and the magic/header field compares inside the ELF struct reader) and substitutes the recorded comparand back into the matching input offsets, manufacturing a header whose parsed section_count exceeds 1; naive lacks I2S and must hit the multi-byte header layout by blind havoc, which it never does in 10 trials.

function: elf.cc:bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::DoReadELFSections(bloaty::RangeSink*, bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ReportSectionsBy)::$_0::operator()(bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ElfFile const&, std::basic_string_view<char, std::char_traits<char> >, unsigned int) const
what_input_feature: The ELF header section-table descriptor bytes (section-header offset / count / entry-size, derived from header fields in the 0x05-0x3f region after the 7f454c46 magic) must form a structurally consistent table so elf.section_count() returns a value greater than 1, making the loop condition i < elf.section_count() true at least once.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry structured header fields (e.g. class/data bytes 02 02 03 at 0x04-0x06 and structured table descriptors around 0x17-0x1f such as 21(!)/98/38/10) and DO take the true side: the blocker branch shows T=40 F=24 in W versus T=0 in L, and ReadSection/Section::header() fire 287000 times in W vs 0 in L, proving section_count()>1 and the loop body executing.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds leave the section-table region filler/uniform (offsets 0x17-0x1f are 20( ) padding or ff/00 runs, e.g. 0x18 is 20( )x10 and 0x1d-0x1f are ff/00 in L), so section_count() collapses to <=1; the loop condition is always false (L1033 T=0 F=57) and section_count() is entered only 222 times vs 286000 in W.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the integer comparisons that validate the section-table header fields, locates the matching operand byte sequence in the input, and substitutes the expected operand at those header offsets, synthesizing a self-consistent section count that naive's blind havoc cannot land on; lineage shows I2S-floor dash rows in the winner's ancestor chain.

what_input_feature: A valid 64-bit ELF header (magic 7f 45 4c 46, ELFCLASS64 = 0x02 at offset 0x04) whose section-header-table fields (e_shoff at 0x28 and e_shnum near 0x3c) parse to a section_count() strictly greater than 1, so the loop guard i < elf.section_count() takes its true side and the body at lines 1194-1239 executes.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds set offset 0x04 to 0x02 (ELFCLASS64) and carry non-trivial header offset/count fields, so ElfFile::section_count() returns ~286000 hits in W vs ~230 in L and the blocker line reports T=16 in W versus T=0 in L; the loop body lines 1194-1239 are W-only.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds use offset 0x04 = 0x01 (ELFCLASS32) with mostly-zeroed or 0x20-padded section-table fields, so section_count() resolves to 0/1, the guard is always false (L1193 shows T=0 F=20), the loop body never runs, and ElfFile::section_count() is hit only ~230 times in L.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one logged CMP operand at input offsets matching the other, letting it splice the precise section-count/offset comparand values into the header so the loop guard comparison passes; naive has only blind havoc and never solves the multi-field header constraint.

what_input_feature: A valid ELF header (7f 45 4c 46 magic) whose e_shoff / e_shnum / e_shentsize section-header-table fields parse to a section count greater than 1, so the loop condition i < elf.section_count() is true on the first iteration and the loop body at lines 1280-1291 executes.
why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seeds carry the ELF magic and structured header bytes that drive section_count() to fire 286000 times in W vs 230 in L, and the blocker true side is taken (T=228000) only in W; ReadSection and Section::header() run 287000 times in W and 0 in L, showing the loop body actually iterates over real sections.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds keep header fields that make section_count() evaluate to <=1, so the loop condition i<section_count() is immediately false: the blocker shows T=0 F=120 in L (loop never enters), Section::header()/ReadSection have 0 L hits, and instead the segment path (Segment::header() 2130000 L hits, GetSegmentName 427000 L hits) is exercised.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the comparand operands of the integer CMPs that the StructReader/section-count parsing performs on the header bytes, splicing the matching section-table field values into the input so section_count() crosses 1; naive has only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection, so it cannot reliably synthesize the correlated header fields.

source_line: case cmsSigXYZData: // Input profile operates in XYZ
what_input_feature: The ICC profile's connection-space signature at byte offset 0x10-0x13 must NOT equal the literal 'XYZ ' (cmsSigXYZData = 0x58595A20). When it spells 'Lab ' (0x4C616220) or another non-XYZ space, the switch on InPCS at line 429 falls through cmsSigXYZData and InPCS takes the false side of the case test, routing into the cmsSigLabData / default arms.
why_winner_satisfies: All winning-resolving seeds carry a non-XYZ PCS literal at 0x10: seed 1/3/5 (cmplog) and seed 2 hold 'Lab' / 'MCH4Lab', and the BYTE DIFF shows W bytes 4c(L)/61(a)/62(b) at offsets 0x10/0x11/0x12 versus L bytes 52(R)/47(G)/42(B). The W per-role overlay reaches the cmsSigLabData arm (lines 454-477) that only executes on the false side of the XYZ case.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has 'RGB XYZ' at 0x10-0x17, so InPCS resolves to cmsSigXYZData and the true side of line 429 is taken (DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows L T=60 F=0 at line 429). The loser never reaches the Lab-input arm and the false branch stays globally blocked.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted profile-signature CMP (the switch on InPCS compiles to integer comparisons against the cmsSigXYZData constant 0x58595A20), finds the matching 'XYZ ' bytes in the input, and substitutes the other observed operand ('Lab '/etc.) at offset 0x10, directly flipping the PCS field. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reliably synthesize the exact 4-byte FOURCC at that offset; value_profile's Hamming-distance feedback gives no monotone path to a discrete FOURCC literal, so both losers stall.

source_line: case cmsSigLabData: // Input profile operates in Lab
what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace/PCS signature in the profile header (offset 0x10 in these seeds) must be the FOURCC 'Lab ' = 0x4c 0x61 0x62 0x20, so InPCS in AddConversion's switch equals cmsSigLabData and the true side at line 452 is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) at offset 0x10-0x13, matching the cmsSigLabData switch constant; the per-role overlay then enters the Lab-input arm (lines 454-478) marked [W].
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds all carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offset 0x10, an RGB/non-PCS device colorspace; InPCS therefore never equals cmsSigLabData and the case-452 true side is never taken (loser hitcount on branch = 0).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the switch comparison against the cmsSigLabData literal, records the constant 'Lab ' in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and substitutes it into the input at the offset where the prior operand ('RGB ' / other signature) appeared; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot directly plant the exact 4-byte magic, so they stall on the false side.

what_input_feature: The ICC header must declare a profile-class / color-space whose InPCS signature is neither cmsSigXYZData nor cmsSigLabData, so the switch(InPCS) at line 427 falls through to the default arm at line 481. In the winner seeds this is driven by the 4-byte class field at offset 0x0c ('link') and the color-space FOURCC at 0x10-0x13 ('MCH7'/'MCH5'/'MCHD'/'MCHC') rather than the loser's 'mntr'+'RGB ' XYZ/Lab-PCS layout.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry the literal bytes 6c 69 6e 6b ('link') at offset 0x0c-0x0f and multi-channel device color-space tags ('MCH7','MCH5','MCHD','MCHC') at 0x10-0x13, which make InPCS resolve to a non-XYZ/non-Lab signature and route execution into the default: arm; the per-role overlay marks line 481 [W] and the winner hits the branch 55 times.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds carry 6d 6e 74 72 ('mntr', a display-device monitor class) at 0x0c-0x0f and 'RGB ' / 'XYZ ' fields at 0x10-0x17, producing an InPCS of cmsSigXYZData so the switch resolves to the XYZ case (line 429) and never reaches the default arm; the winner true-hitcount is 55 while the loser is 0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which value_profile lacks. The InPCS dispatch is a switch over exact 4-byte signature constants; value_profile's distance-bucket feedback gives no smooth path toward an unrelated FOURCC, but I2SRandReplace logs the compared signature constant and splices it directly into the matching input-byte position, planting the exact non-PCS color-space token that drives execution to the default arm.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature at input offset 0x14 must be the FOURCC 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20) while the PCS/colorspace field at 0x10 reads 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20), so the two compared colorspace signatures equal cmsSigXYZData and cmsSigLabData respectively, satisfying the compound equality gate at line 502.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal FOURCC bytes that make a==cmsSigXYZData && b==cmsSigLabData; the per-role overlay shows the true side of line 502 taken 20 times in W (T=20) and the on-branch hitcount is 121-187, whereas loser seeds never take it.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at 0x10 and 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20) at 0x14 in the opposite slots, so a==cmsSigRGBData not cmsSigXYZData; line 502's true side is never taken (T=0) and the branch stays blocked 24h.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the integer/string CMP comparing the parsed colorspace signature against the literals cmsSigXYZData and cmsSigLabData, logs the constant operand, finds the seed bytes matching the other operand, and substitutes the exact 4-byte FOURCC into the input — naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the exact 4-byte literal by chance, so they stay blocked all 10 trials.

Only then does line 551 evaluate (CurrentColorSpace != cmsSigXYZData) && (CurrentColorSpace != cmsSigLabData) to TRUE.

what_input_feature: An ICC parametric curve whose stored Type selector decodes to the integer 2, so the switch(Type) at line 344 dispatches to case 2 (line 380). The blocker is the true side of the case-2 dispatch; the input must carry a parametricCurveType field exactly equal to 2 somewhere in the parsed profile bytes.

what_input_feature: Input bytes parsed into an ICC profile that selects parametric curve Type 4 (case 4, sRGB) and supplies Params[4]/Params[1]/Params[2] coefficients such that for some sample R>=Params[4] the linear form e = Params[1]*R + Params[2] evaluates to <= 0, taking the False side of the e>0 gate at cmsgamma.c:497. The structural precondition is a magic-byte-laden profile header (acsp 'acspAPPL', profile-class/colorspace tags) whose downstream tag table points at a Type-4 parametricCurveType.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile header whose data-colorspace / PCS FOURCC fields at offsets 0x10-0x17 carry signatures such as 'Lab Lab ' or 'MCH3XYZ ' (rather than 'RGB XYZ '), driving construction of a segmented tone curve (nSegments>0) whose per-segment domain test (R > x0 && R <= x1) fails for every segment, so EvalSegmentedFn runs the descending loop to exhaustion and takes the i<0 (false) loop-exit side at line 721.

what_input_feature: A small, structurally-valid ICC profile header where parsing reaches MemorySeek with an offset greater than ResData->Size. The cmplog CMP buffer logs the magic/header comparand bytes (e.g. the device-class and signature fields the parser compares against ICC constants), and I2SRandReplace splices those exact bytes into the input at the matching offsets, letting vpc assemble a well-formed small-buffer header that drives parsing to an out-of-bounds seek. This is what installs the exact header/tag literals (magic, size, color-space fourccs) that naive/value_profile must hit by blind havoc; both winning sides are cmplog/vpc (the two I2S-delta pairs) and their lineage carries I2S-floor dash rows.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte PCS color-space signature in the ICC profile header (offset 0x14-0x17) must be the literal 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) so that cmsGetPCS(hProfile) == cmsSigLabData; any other PCS such as 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20) takes the false branch.

why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed carries 58 59 5a 20 ('XYZ ') at offsets 0x14-0x16, so cmsGetPCS returns a non-Lab PCS and the true branch is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the PCS-signature integer/string comparison, log the runtime operand cmsSigLabData, and I2SRandReplace splices that 4-byte literal into the input at the offset where the old PCS value appeared; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly mutate the 4-byte field to the exact magic, which they fail to do in 24h.

source_line: if (cmsIsTag(hProfile, tag16)) { // Is there any LUT-Based table?
what_input_feature: The ICC profile body must declare a tag-table entry whose 4-byte tag signature equals the runtime-selected tag16 (Device2PCS16[Intent], a LUT-based AToBx FOURCC). cmsIsTag walks the parsed tag directory comparing each stored signature against tag16, so the input bytes encoding a directory entry must contain that exact FOURCC.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry valid ICC header bytes (acsp magic, lcms signature, device-class FOURCCs like prtr/spac/mntr and colorspace tags RGB/CMY/Lab/XYZ) and the cmplog winner's ancestor chain shows I2S-floor dash rows, indicating I2SRandReplace spliced the logged tag16 FOURCC into a tag-table offset so the stored signature matches.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds (naive / value_profile) all show the false side: at line 355 L takes T=0 F=48, i.e. no stored tag signature ever equals tag16. Their havoc/token mutators never reconstruct the precise 4-byte FOURCC needed in the tag directory, so cmsIsTag stays false.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/value_profile_cmplog intercept the integer/string CMP inside cmsIsTag's signature comparison, log the expected tag16 FOURCC, and I2SRandReplace substitutes that exact 4-byte value into the input at a tag-directory offset. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot land the exact magic, so they stay blocked.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte profile-connection-space (PCS) FOURCC at input offsets 0x14-0x17 must be the literal 'Lab ' (0x4c 0x61 0x62 0x20). cmsGetPCS returns cmsSigLabData only for that exact tag, gating the call to ChangeInterpolationToTrilinear at cmsio1.c:776/624, which is the only path that executes the blocker line 526.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries 'Lab' at offsets 0x14-0x16 (4c 61 62), supplied by the value_profile_cmplog winner; the BYTE DIFF shows these three offsets are concentrated to a single W value distinct from the L value, exactly the exact-equality FOURCC the cmsGetPCS==cmsSigLabData gate requires.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds (value_profile) hold 'XYZ' (58 59 5a) at offsets 0x14-0x16, so cmsGetPCS returns cmsSigXYZData, line 775's equality fails, ChangeInterpolationToTrilinear is never called, and the blocker false side at line 526 is never reached (loser resolved=0/10, blocked=10).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile. The cmsGetPCS check compares the input's 0x14-0x17 word against the constant cmsSigLabData ('Lab '); cmplog logs that comparison and I2SRandReplace splices the logged 'Lab ' literal directly into the input bytes, flipping 'XYZ'->'Lab ' in one step. value_profile's CMP_MAP only buckets Hamming distance and cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte FOURCC, so it stays blocked.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte profile-header signature field driving cmsGetPCS (around offsets 0x10-0x13, the colorspace/PCS FOURCC) must equal a non-Lab signature value (e.g. 'Lab ' replaced by 'XYZ'/'MCH7'/'MCHC'/'3CLR'-style FOURCCs), so the equality comparison cmsGetPCS(hProfile)==cmsSigLabData evaluates false; the loser keeps the Lab-matching FOURCC and is pinned on the true side.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving vpc seed carries a varied, non-uniform FOURCC at offsets 0x10-0x13 ('Lab ', 'MCH7', 'MCHC', '3CLR') and reaches the blocker's false side (line 775 W shows T=9 F=10), evidence the PCS comparand was driven off the Lab literal.
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking value_profile seeds hold the identical uniform bytes 52 47 42 20 ('RGB ') at 0x10-0x13 and PCS resolves to Lab, so they always take the true side (line 775 L shows T=20 F=0) and never resolve the false branch.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: the CmpLogObserver records the runtime operands of cmsGetPCS()==cmsSigLabData, and I2SRandReplace splices the logged comparand into the input FOURCC bytes, flipping the equality off Lab in one mutation. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets a CMP_MAP Hamming bucket, which cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte substitution to clear the gate.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile header whose FOURCC-encoded fields differ from the loser's: device-class FOURCC 'prtr' (70 72 74 72) at offset 0x0c instead of 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72), version byte 04 at 0x08 instead of 03, and concentrated header bytes f0 at 0x2d and 50 at 0x3d. These header values steer cmsIsMatrixShaper so the cmsSigRedColorantTag lookup at line 816 yields the FALSE side.

why_loser_doesnt: All five loser-blocking seeds carry 'mntr' at offset 0x0c-0x0f, version 03 40 at 0x08-0x09, and 00 at 0x3d; they only ever take the TRUE side of the blocker (L 816 T=31 F=0), never reaching the false branch.

The ICC parser performs equality comparisons against FOURCC literals (device class / tag signatures); cmplog logs those comparand pairs and I2SRandReplace splices the logged values into the matching input-byte offsets, letting vpc reach a header configuration where the colorant-tag lookup returns FALSE — a substitution value_profile's gradient feedback alone never lands.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte ICC profile device-class field at input offset 0x0c-0x0f must hold a class whose profile triggers cmsIsCLUT()==TRUE (e.g. 'prtr', 'spac', 'pamc'), distinct from the loser's uniformly-present 'mntr' class. This is a magic-FOURCC equality gate inside cmsIsCLUT reached from cmsIsIntentSupported line 868.
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries a non-'mntr' device-class FOURCC at 0x0c-0x0f: 'prtr', 'spac', and the byte-diff shows 0x0c set to 70(p)/73(s) and 0x0d set to 72(r)/70(p) in winners. The vpc-discovered seeds drive the blocker true side (hit_div=266) and all 10 winner trials resolve.
why_loser_doesnt: All 10 loser-blocking seeds carry exactly 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) at offset 0x0c-0x0f and never vary it; the byte diff shows loser offset 0x0c is 6d(m)x10 and 0x0d is 6e(n)x10 with zero variation, so cmsIsCLUT never returns TRUE and the true branch stays blocked across all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile's CMP_MAP-only signal. I2SRandReplace splices the device-class comparand FOURCC logged at the cmsIsCLUT equality check back into the input bytes at the offset where the old FOURCC sat, flipping 'mntr' to a CLUT-bearing class in one step; value_profile has no I2S mutator and only gets a Hamming-distance bucket, which does not place the exact FOURCC at the right offset.

what_input_feature: Header signature bytes at offsets 0x0c-0x16 (profile-class + input/output colorspace fourcc) that decode into a color-transform pipeline whose stage signature sequence (mpe->Type) does NOT equal the four-element expected list cmsSigCurveSetElemType/cmsSigMatrixElemType/cmsSigMatrixElemType/cmsSigCurveSetElemType passed via va_arg at the cmsopt.c:1690 call site, so that mpe->Type != Type evaluates true.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds vary the profile-class/colorspace fourccs (e.g. 'link'/'lams', 'XYZ '/'CMY '/'MCH3', 'Lab '/'RGB ') at 0x0c-0x16, producing pipelines whose first stage Type diverges from the expected cmsSig*ElemType comparand; the blocker true side is hit (W: T=13 F=4) and resolves 10/10.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are uniformly locked to profile-class 'mntr' with colorspaces 'RGB ' and 'XYZ ' at 0x0c-0x16, building exactly the shaper-matrix-matrix-shaper pipeline the function expects, so mpe->Type == Type at every iteration and the true (mismatch) side is never taken (L: T=0 F=30), blocking 10/10.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which value_profile lacks. The cmplog CMP buffer records the runtime stage-signature comparand (Type) at cmslut.c:129; I2SRandReplace splices that logged 4-byte signature into the input header bytes, flipping the colorspace/class fourcc so a differently-typed stage is constructed and the mismatch branch fires. Plain value_profile only gets a coarse Hamming-distance bucket, which does not place the exact signature into the input.

what_input_feature: A parsed ICC profile whose CLUT element supplies a per-axis grid-point count clutPoints[b-1] of 0 or 1, so dim<=1 holds inside CubeSize. The winning seeds carry alternate profile-class/colorspace tags (e.g. 'lams'/'link', '8CLR', 'MCH8Lab') and a flipped flag byte 01 at offset 0x2d that route to an MPE CLUT stage with a degenerate dimension, whereas a normal 'mntr'/'RGB ' display profile fills full-size dimensions.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving vpc seeds reach the blocker true side: at line 470 the per-role overlay shows the call-chain branch with W counts T=7 F=18 (true side hit), and the caller's n==0 path (lines 577-580) is W-only, meaning CubeSize returned 0 because a dimension was <=1. The vpc seeds differ at the colorspace/tag offsets (0x0c-0x16) and at 0x2d=01, structuring a profile that allocates a CLUT with a unit/zero axis.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser value_profile seeds never take the true side: at line 470 L counts are T=0 F=120, i.e. the dim<=1 test is reached 120 times but always falls through with dim>1. The loser seeds hold the standard 'lcms'/'mntr'/'RGB ' display-profile tags and 00 at 0x2d, producing full-size CLUT dimensions so dim never drops to <=1.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers in the pair share value_profile's distance-bucket comparison feedback; the only delta is value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace stage. The degenerate dimension byte is a discrete small comparand reached only after the colorspace/tag bytes pass earlier equality CMPs; I2SRandReplace splices logged comparand values into the exact input-byte positions, letting vpc set the tag/dimension bytes that drive dim<=1, which value_profile without I2S substitution cannot synthesize.

what_input_feature: The header must carry specific 4-byte literals at the profile signature/color-space/PCS tag fields (offsets 0x05, 0x0c-0x16) that lcms compares against constants while parsing the ICC profile, so a CLUT (LUT) stage is constructed but its Tab.T table pointer ends up NULL — making CLutElemTypeFree take the false branch at cmslut.c:534.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the exact byte set the parser CMPs demand: offset 0x05='a'(61), the color-space tag bytes 0x0c-0x0f='link', the 0x10-0x13 PCS field='8CLR'/variants, and 0x2d=01 — these drive parsing to a CLUT stage with an empty (NULL) table, so 'if (Data -> Tab.T)' is false (avg hitcount on winner=9, resolved 8/10).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds hold the alternative constants ('c'(63) at 0x05, 'mntr' at 0x0c-0x0f, 'RGB ' at 0x10-0x13, 0x2d=00); they never reach the CLUT-with-NULL-table configuration, so the false branch is never taken (resolved 0/10, hitcount 0, blocked 24h).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback. CmpLogObserver records the exact comparand constants the lcms tag/signature CMPs expect, and I2SRandReplace splices those logged literals back into the matching input-byte positions, jumping the gate in one mutation. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and can only hill-climb via Hamming/prefix CMP_MAP buckets, which does not land the exact multi-byte tag literals here within budget.

what_input_feature: The ICC header must carry specific magic-valued fields: a profile-class FOURCC at offset 0x0c-0x0f that is one of spac/prtr/link (not mntr), plus the discriminator bytes 0x2d=0x01 and 0x3d=0x50, which together steer the profile-creation path into a CLUT stage invoked with a non-NULL Table argument so cmsStageAllocCLut16bitGranular takes the true branch at line 589.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner seeds carry exact magic values at the diff offsets: 0x2d=0x01 and 0x3d=0x50 in every winner, and the class FOURCC at 0x0c is spac/prtr/link; the W role hits lines 590-592 (the Table-copy loop) confirming the true branch is taken.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser seed has 0x2d=0x00 and 0x3d=0x00 and the class FOURCC fixed at mntr, never matching the magic combination; the L role takes the false branch (L 589 T=0 F=20) and never reaches the Table-copy body.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which logs the runtime CMP operand (the expected magic FOURCC / header constant) and splices it directly into the matching input bytes; value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets a CMP_MAP distance bucket, which cannot place the exact multi-byte magic constant, so it never flips these discrete equality gates.

what_input_feature: A valid ICC profile header (lcms/acsp magic) whose 4-byte profile-connection-space (PCS) signature at offset 0x14-0x16 spells 'Lab' rather than 'XYZ'. The Lab PCS routes lcms to build a pipeline that contains an MPE stage whose DupElemPtr function pointer is NULL, so cmsStageDup at cmslut.c:1258 takes the (globally-blocked) false branch and executes NewMPE->Data = NULL.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the bytes 4c 61 62 ('Lab') at offsets 0x14-0x16 and the false-branch-only lines 1270-1271 (NewMPE->Data = NULL) are marked [W]; the winner resolves the branch in 10/10 trials with hitcount 82 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: All five loser-blocking seeds carry 58 59 5a ('XYZ') at offsets 0x14-0x16, an XYZ PCS that builds only stages whose DupElemPtr is non-NULL; the loser blocks 10/10 trials with hitcount 0 and never reaches line 1270.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer where lcms's signature-field comparison logged the operands, then substitutes the matching operand bytes (the 'Lab' PCS string) into the input at the offset where the seed already held the comparand. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must stumble on the exact 4-byte 'Lab' substitution by blind havoc byte mutation alone, which never lands in 24h.

what_input_feature: Header tag bytes at offsets 0x0c-0x0f (device class) and 0x10-0x17 (data/PCS colorspace signatures) set to non-default four-char codes (e.g. 'link','spac','scnr' with 'MCHC'/'GRAY'/'Lab ') so the constructed cmsPipeline contains adjacent stages where next->InputChannels != prev->OutputChannels, reaching the true (return FALSE) side of cmslut.c:1305.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog winning seeds carry varied class codes at 0x0c-0x0f ('link','prtr','spac','scnr') and varied colorspaces at 0x10-0x17 ('MCHCXYZ ','GRAYXYZ ','RGB Lab '), driving heterogeneous channel-count pipeline stages; the per-role overlay marks line 1306 'return FALSE' as W-only and the branch hitcount is 107 for the winner.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser seeds are byte-locked to class 'mntr' at 0x0c-0x0f and colorspace 'RGB XYZ ' at 0x10-0x17 across all 10 seeds, building channel-consistent pipelines that always take the false side (T=0 F=670 at L1305) and never resolve the branch (resolved=0/10).
mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between value_profile_cmplog and value_profile is the I2SRandReplace input-to-state mutator. The four-char ICC signature codes ('link','MCHC','GRAY','Lab ') are exact magic-string comparands tested in lcms profile dispatch; I2SRandReplace logs the runtime CMP operand and splices the exact literal into the input bytes, letting vpc populate non-'mntr'/'RGB'/'XYZ' tag fields that select differently-shaped pipelines. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and its CMP_MAP Hamming gradient cannot synthesize the exact four-char tag, so it stays pinned to the default class.

what_input_feature: Profile-header FOURCC bytes at offsets 0x0c-0x13 (device-class signature + colorspace) set to a value that makes cmsIsTag(hProfile, tagFloat) true, so _cmsReadFloatDevicelinkTag runs and invokes cmsPipelineDup(cmsReadTag(hProfile, tagFloat)); when that float tag read returns NULL, the blocker's TRUE side (lut == NULL) is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry varied non-monitor class signatures at 0x0c-0x13 (e.g. 'abst', 'link', 'MCH6'/'MCH9') and the W overlay uniquely hits the float-tag caller _cmsReadFloatDevicelinkTag at cmsio1.c:665-667; on the call chain the winner takes the TRUE side of 'if (cmsIsTag(hProfile, tagFloat))' (W T=2 F=0) and then reaches L1461 with T=10 F=0.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser seed holds the constant 'mntr'/'RGB ' (monitor RGB) FOURCC at 0x0c-0x13, never satisfying the float-tag predicate (L cmsIsTag(tagFloat) T=0 F=30); the loser instead reaches cmsPipelineDup with a non-NULL lut and takes the FALSE side at L1461 (L T=0 F=40), proceeding into the duplication loop.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile; the cmsIsTag/tag-table comparisons against the float-devicelink tag signature and the profile-class checks are equality CMPs whose comparands are logged by CmpLogObserver and spliced into the matching input bytes, letting vpc synthesize the exact FOURCC that routes into the float path. value_profile only buckets comparison distance but never performs the I2SRandReplace substitution of the literal, so it cannot land the discrete signature.

what_input_feature: Specific 4-byte ICC header signature bytes: the profile/device-class field at offset 0x0c-0x0f set to 'link' (6c 69 6e 6b) instead of 'mntr', plus the flag byte at 0x2d=0x01 instead of 0x00. These steer the parser down the LUT-A2B reading path where cmsPipelineInsertStage is called with an mpe argument that is NULL (ReadSetOfCurves returns NULL), taking the blocked TRUE side of the NULL check.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds (value_profile_cmplog) carry 'link' at 0x0c-0x0f and 0x2d=0x01; the 1-hop caller block shows the whole Type_LUTA2B_Read path (lines 2571-2588) hit only by W [W] markers, and the d=1 divergent branch shows the TRUE side (T=11) reached only on the W role.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds (value_profile) keep 'mntr' at 0x0c-0x0f and 0x2d=0x00, so the LUT-A2B reader / NULL-mpe path is never structured correctly; the d=1 branch row shows L took only the FALSE side (F=310, T=0) and never the blocked TRUE side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which splices logged 4-byte CMP operands (the ICC signature constants 'link', flag 0x01) directly into the matching input-byte offsets. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot reliably land these exact multi-byte signature words via input-to-state substitution and stays on the FALSE side.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte ICC profile device-class signature at offsets 0x0c-0x0f must spell a class that drives DefaultICCintents down a path producing a pipeline with l1->Elements==NULL and l2->Elements==NULL at cmsPipelineCat (e.g. 'link' = 0x6c 0x69 0x6e 0x6b, or 'prtr'), rather than the monitor class 'mntr' that all loser seeds carry.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry a non-monitor device class at 0x0c-0x0f: 'link' (6c 69 6e 6b) and 'prtr' (70 72 74 72); the BYTE DIFF shows W bytes 70(p)/6c(l) and 72(r)/69(i) at 0x0c-0x0d versus L's constant 6d(m)/6e(n), confirming W explores classes that reach the empty-LUT true branch (hit_div=57, T-side taken).
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has 'mntr' (6d 6e 74 72) at 0x0c-0x0f; at 0x0c the L set is 6d(m)x10 and at 0x0d it is 6e(n)x10 with zero variation, so the loser never reaches a class whose pipeline has NULL Elements, leaving the true side blocked (L1609 T=0 F=30).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace splices the cmplog-logged 4-byte device-class comparand into the input bytes at the offset where it appears, flipping 'mntr' to a class signature (e.g. 'link'/'prtr') that the parser compares against; plain value_profile has only CMP_MAP distance feedback and havoc, with no I2SRandReplace mutator to perform the exact 4-byte substitution.

what_input_feature: The input must be an ICC profile whose header signature fields are substituted to exact magic-string values: a profile-class/colorspace tag region around offsets 0x0c-0x0f and the colorspace/PCS signatures at 0x10-0x16 (e.g. 'CMY '/'Lab ' or 'Lab '/'Lab ' rather than 'RGB '/'XYZ '). These exact 4-byte FOURCC values steer pipeline construction down the XYZ2Lab/Lab2XYZ optimization path so that _Remove2Op is invoked and its element list reaches the *pt1 == NULL state, taking the True branch.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry colorspace bytes 'CMY '/'Lab ' or 'Lab '/'Lab ' at 0x10-0x16 and 'prtr'/zeroed class bytes at 0x0c-0x0f, and the winner hits the blocker True side 63 times (T=63 F=153) while invoking the deeper optimization machinery (XFormSampler16 W=35900 vs L=0).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds all carry 'mntr' at 0x0c-0x0f and the fixed 'RGB '/'XYZ ' signature at 0x10-0x16; they never take the True side of the blocker (L: T=0 F=924) — the fixed RGB/XYZ profile keeps execution off the Lab-conversion optimization path that drives _Remove2Op to the NULL-element state.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of value_profile. The colorspace/PCS FOURCC fields are compared against hard-coded constants during profile parsing; cmplog logs those comparison operands and I2SRandReplace splices the exact 4-byte values into the matching input offsets, flipping the header from RGB/XYZ to the Lab-conversion signatures in a single targeted substitution. value_profile lacks this mutator and must hit the 4-byte literals by chance, which it never does in 10 trials.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile header with byte-exact magic and colorspace signature FOURCCs (offsets ~0x0c-0x16: e.g. profile-class/colorspace fields differing from the loser's 'mntr'/'RGB XYZ' layout) that pass header validation, get optimized, and reach _MultiplyMatrix while *pt1 (Lut->Elements) is NULL, taking the true side at cmsopt.c:196.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving vpc seeds carry distinct FOURCC content at the diverging offsets (e.g. 0x10-0x16 'CMY'/'Lab', 0x0c-0x0f 'prtr'/zeros) produced by CMP-guided substitution; the per-role overlay shows W reaches L196 with T=14 (true branch taken) where L only reaches F=154, so the winner exercises the empty-Elements true-return path.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds are stuck on a single 'mntr'/'RGB XYZ' header layout and never produce the byte-exact field combination that drives the pipeline to an empty Elements list; at L196 the loser shows T=0 F=154, i.e. it always falls through the false side and never resolves the true branch in 10/10 blocked trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile: the CmpLogObserver buffers the exact comparand FOURCC/magic values the parser compares against and I2SRandReplace splices them back into the matching input offsets, letting the winner satisfy the header-signature equality CMPs that gate reaching _MultiplyMatrix with the required pipeline state. value_profile only has the CMP_MAP distance gradient and no substitution mutator, so it cannot place the exact bytes.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile header whose device/profile-class FOURCC (offset 0x0c-0x0f) and colorspace tags (0x10-0x16) parse into a transform pipeline that yields two adjacent matrix stages, both with Offset==NULL and 3x3 channel dims, so _MultiplyMatrix reaches the matrix-merge guard at line 211. The winner reaches the line at all (T=13 vs L T=0); the false-side then fires because offsets are NULL/dims are 3x3.
why_winner_satisfies: Winning vpc seeds carry diverse, well-formed profile-class FOURCCs at 0x0c-0x0f (prtr, scnr, lcms-class) and varied colorspace pairs (CMY/XYZ, RGB/Lab, MCH3/XYZ) at 0x10-0x16, building pipelines that survive to the matrix-multiply optimizer. The branch goes from never reached by the loser to T=13 hits, and the function entry-line proxy XFormSampler16 shows 107000 W hits vs 0 L hits.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser vp seeds are uniformly stuck at the mntr profile class (0x0c=6d 'm', 0x0d=6e 'n', flagged DIFFER) with a single fixed RGB/XYZ colorspace, never producing the dual-matrix-stage pipeline; the blocker line is reached on the false direction only (T=0 F=0 then T=0 F=10) and the matrix-merge path stays at 0 hits across all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: vpc adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that vp lacks. I2SRandReplace splices logged ICC FOURCC comparison operands (the magic tag/profile-class strings the parser compares the header against) directly into the input bytes at the offsets where they are read, manufacturing valid profile-class/colorspace combinations that vp's distance-bucket feedback alone could not assemble byte-by-byte.

what_input_feature: A valid ICC color-space FOURCC signature in the InputFormat/OutputFormat header fields (offsets 0x10-0x16: e.g. 'Lab '/'Lab ' rather than 'RGB '/'XYZ ') such that _cmsICCcolorSpace((int)T_COLORSPACE(*InputFormat)) maps to a recognized cmsColorSpaceSignature != 0, so the line-668 check (ColorSpace == 0 || OutputColorSpace == 0) is FALSE and execution continues into resampling.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the exact color-space token bytes the equality CMPs in _cmsICCcolorSpace expect; cmplog/vpc logged the constant operand of each color-space comparison and I2SRandReplace substituted it into the header offsets, producing seeds whose ColorSpace lookup succeeds (avg hitcount on the false side 53/71, resolved 10/10).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry header bytes ('RGB '/'XYZ ' at 0x10-0x16) that, in this trial population, drive the lookup to a value that fails the line-668 guard so they take the TRUE side and return FALSE early; naive/value_profile have no I2S substitution to plant the matching color-space FOURCC and stay blocked 10/10 for 24h.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog both add I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the color-space comparison operands, and splices the matching FOURCC into the input header. naive and plain value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they cannot place the exact color-space token by directed substitution. This is the I2S roadblock-solving mechanism, matching claimed_mechanism I2SRandReplace.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile carrying a tone-curve (Curve1) whose cmsEvalToneCurveFloat output for at least one of the 256 samples is >= 131072.0, so the comparison `if (y < 131072.0)` takes its false side. Reaching this requires a well-formed profile header (`acsp`/`mntr` magic, valid colorspace tag e.g. Lab/XYZ) plus curve-tag bytes whose decoded float magnitude is enormous.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds reach the curve evaluation and take the false branch 5580/5599 times (W shows F=5580 on the blocker), and the BYTE DIFF shows winners carrying alternate colorspace/tag bytes (4c 'L', 61 'a', 62 'b' at offsets 0x14-0x16) and a `63 'c'` at 0x2c absent in losers, i.e. a distinct parsed-profile configuration that drives the curve into the large-output regime.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds execute the curve evaluation but the false side is never taken (L shows T=15300 F=0 on the blocker); their bytes hold only the unmodified `58 'X' 59 'Y' 5a 'Z'` colorspace and zeros at the differentiating offsets, so the tone curve always evaluates below 131072.0.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc carry I2SRandReplace, which reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver and substitutes one comparison operand for the other at matching input offsets, planting the exact tag/curve bytes whose decoded float clears the 131072.0 magnitude threshold; the two winners (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) both add I2S over their non-I2S sibling (naive, value_profile), so the divergence is attributable to the I2S technique delta.

what_input_feature: The ICC profile header / colorspace fields must be arranged so the runtime InputFormat and OutputFormat both carry a channel count of exactly 3 (T_CHANNELS==3), satisfying the RGB-to-RGB precondition at cmsopt.c:1673. This depends on the colorspace-signature bytes at offsets 0x10-0x13 and the format-encoding bytes around 0x09-0x0d resolving to a 3-channel format rather than a non-3-channel one.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seeds carry varied colorspace signatures at offsets 0x10-0x13 (e.g. CLR/MCH2/GRAY) together with the profile-class bytes ff ff ff / prtr at 0x09-0x0d that, after the channel-count CMP is solved by I2S substitution, yield T_CHANNELS==3 and take the blocked True path 10/0 in the DIVERGENT BRANCHES W column.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds are all stuck on a 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) colorspace tag with 40 00 00 mntr format bytes at 0x09-0x0d and never flip the channel-count comparison; every loser trial stays on the False side (T=0 F=10), so the matrix-shaper RGB-to-RGB gate is never opened.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: cmplog buffers the channel-count comparison operands during execution and splices the exact 3-channel format encoding back into the input bytes, deterministically passing the T_CHANNELS(*InputFormat)!=3 / T_CHANNELS(*OutputFormat)!=3 equality gate that value_profile's CMP_MAP Hamming-distance feedback alone never solves.

what_input_feature: The profile colorspace/PCS signature bytes at input offsets 0x10-0x16 must encode a value whose derived InputFormat passes _cmsFormatterIs8bit. Winners carry the 4-byte ASCII run 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) at both 0x10 and 0x14; losers carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) and 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20). The blocker on line 1676 demands an 8-bit InputFormat, which only the winner's colorspace fields produce.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds hold 4c 61 62 (Lab) at offsets 0x10-0x12 and 0x14-0x16 (BYTE DIFF shows 0x10=4c, 0x11=61, 0x12=62, 0x14=4c, 0x15=61, 0x16=62), and the cmplog/vpc winners discovered the branch in 1.3-2.3h, resolving it in 10/10 trials with avg hitcount 53-71 on the true side.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds hold 52 47 42 (RGB) at 0x10-0x12 and 58 59 5a (XYZ) at 0x14-0x16 (BYTE DIFF DIFFER rows), so InputFormat is not 8-bit; line 1676 takes the false (return FALSE) side in all 10 trials, blocked 24.00h with hitcount 0 on the true side.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the integer/string CMPs inside _cmsFormatterIs8bit's format derivation, logs the expected 8-bit-formatter operand, finds the colorspace byte run in the input and substitutes the matching value at offsets 0x10-0x16. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and never planted the exact colorspace bytes that yield an 8-bit InputFormat.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile whose header fields steer the transform into a pipeline that PreOptimize() can fully collapse to an identity, so that after element removal (*PtrLut)->Elements becomes NULL. Concretely the winner sets the color-space tags to CMY/Lab (offsets 0x10-0x16 = 43 4d 59 20 4c 61 62 = 'CMY Lab'), a 'lams' magic variant (offset 0x05 = 'a' vs loser 'c'), and byte 0x2d = 01 vs loser 00.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 'CMY Lab' at offsets 0x10-0x16, the 'a' at offset 0x05 ('lams'), and 0x2d=01; these route through a pipeline that PreOptimize reduces to identity, taking the L1935 true branch 7 times (T=7) while loser never does (T=0). The winning side of the blocker is reached only after PreOptimize at L1932 removes every element.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds instead carry 'RGB XYZ' at offsets 0x10-0x16, 'c' at offset 0x05 ('lcms'/'mntr'), and 0x2d=00; their pipeline still has elements after PreOptimize, so L1935 stays on the false branch (T=0 F=91) and the optimize-to-identity short-circuit is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's feedback. I2SRandReplace splices CMP-logged comparand values (the ICC color-space / magic FOURCC literals lcms compares header fields against) directly into the matching input-byte offsets, planting the exact 'CMY'/'Lab' tags and magic-variant bytes that route to the collapsible pipeline. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot place those multi-byte literals atomically and never resolves the branch.

what_input_feature: The ICC profile header's data-colorspace/PCS fields at offsets 0x10-0x16 must hold the four-char signatures 'Lab ' (0x4c 0x61 0x62) for both colorspace and PCS, instead of 'RGB '/'XYZ '. These bytes drive Type/channel computation so dwInput no longer equals (dwInput & ~Mask)==Type for any of the matching InputFormatters16 entries, causing the loop at L3516 to run to completion and take the blocked false side (loop exit, break at L3525, then fall through to L3543).
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry 4c 61 62 20 (Lab ) at offsets 0x10-0x12 and again at 0x14-0x16, a colorspace pairing that does not satisfy the L3519 equality for any 16-bit input formatter, so the for-loop scans all entries (W: T=900 F=20) and the false/exit side is reached.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 52 47 42 20 (RGB ) at 0x10-0x12 and 58 59 5a 20 (XYZ ) at 0x14-0x16; this colorspace matches an InputFormatters16 entry at L3519, so the loop returns early (L3520-3522) before ever exhausting the entries (L: T=340 F=0), never taking the false branch.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the L3519 comparison's operands (the table entry's Type and the runtime-derived dwInput, plus the underlying 4-char signature compares in cmsGetColorSpace/header parsing) are logged, locates the matching colorspace bytes in the input, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets — flipping the signature toward a value that misses every InputFormatters16 row and forces the loop-exit (false) side. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace; value_profile's CMP_MAP only rewards Hamming-closer operands, which biases toward MATCHING a formatter (taking true) rather than exhaustively missing all of them, so neither loser ever reaches the false side in 10 trials.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte data-colorspace signature in the ICC profile header (offset 0x10-0x13) must be a value that cmsGetColorSpace returns and that cmsChannelsOfColorSpace does not recognize, so nOutputChans is negative (< 0). Supported tags like 'RGB ' yield a positive channel count and miss the branch; an unsupported/unmapped signature drives nOutputChans negative.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry varied non-RGB colorspace tags at offset 0x10-0x16 ('MDHC'/'XYZ ', 'Luvl'/'Lab ', 'B.LR') and differing profile-class bytes at 0x0c-0x0f ('prtr' or zeroed vs 'mntr'), producing a ColorSpace whose channel lookup returns negative; the branch (nOutputChans < 0) TRUE side is taken (winner hitcount 3 on branch, resolved 9/10).
why_loser_doesnt: All five loser seeds carry the identical fixed signature '52 47 42 20' ("RGB ") at offset 0x10-0x13 and 'mntr' at 0x0c-0x0f, a fully supported colorspace whose channel count is positive, so nOutputChans < 0 is always FALSE; loser blocked 10/10 with branch hitcount 0.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback. lcms compares the parsed colorspace signature against a fixed table of supported FOURCC literals; I2SRandReplace logs those comparison operands and splices the literal directly into the input bytes at the signature offset, letting vpc reach unsupported-signature values that flip nOutputChans negative. value_profile, having only Hamming-bucket gradient feedback and no I2S substitution, stays anchored to the seed's 'RGB ' signature.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte color-space signature field at file offset 0x10-0x13 must be the ASCII FourCC 'GRAY' (47 52 41 59). cmsGetColorSpace reads this field and only 'GRAY' makes Space==cmsSigGrayData, selecting the case at cmspcs.c:727 (the globally-blocked true side).
why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries 'GRAY' (47 52 41 59) at offsets 0x10-0x13 and the W column hits the cmsSigGrayData arm at L727; loser-blocking seeds never do.
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13, so Space==cmsSigRgbData; they take the L732 RgbData arm and the cmsSigGrayData true side at L727 stays unhit (T=0).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which copies the exact 4-byte constant compared at runtime (the 'GRAY' signature literal) into the input position where the seed's color-space field appears. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets a CMP_MAP distance bucket, which gives no direct path to the exact 4-byte FourCC, so it never flips 'RGB ' to 'GRAY'.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte data-colorspace signature at offset 0x10-0x13 of the ICC header must be a non-RGB FOURCC (e.g. 'Lab ', 'CMY ', 'MCH1', 'MCH4', 'XYZ') rather than 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20), so _cmsEndPointsBySpace's switch on Space does not enter the cmsSigRgbData case and instead reaches the Lab/CMYK/CMY/default arms, taking the FALSE (non-RGB) side of the blocker at cmspcs.c:732.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry varied non-RGB colorspace tags at offset 0x10-0x13: 'CMY ', 'Lab ', 'MCH1', 'MCH4' (W bytes at 0x10 are 4c/43/4d/35 = L/C/M/5, never the loser's uniform 52='R'), so the dispatch lands on a later case arm and exits the RGB arm via its false side.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has the colorspace field fixed to 'RGB ' (offset 0x10-0x13 = 52 47 42 20, 20/20 occurrences), so Space == cmsSigRgbData always holds, the RGB switch arm is entered (true side), and the blocked false side at cmspcs.c:732 is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the integer/string CMP that dispatches the switch on the colorspace signature, log the runtime operand (the cmsSig* FOURCC constants such as cmsSigLabData/cmsSigCmykData) into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes those logged constants into the input at the offset where the current 'RGB ' tag appears. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly mutate a 4-byte field into a valid alternate FOURCC, which never happens in 10 trials.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte color-space signature read into `Space` (input offset 0x10-0x13) must NOT equal any of the recognized switch cases (cmsSigGrayData, cmsSigRgbData='RGB ', cmsSigLabData, cmsSigCmykData, cmsSigCmyData); only a non-matching signature falls through to the `default:;` arm at line 752.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry non-standard FOURCCs at offset 0x10-0x13 — e.g. 4d 43 48 35 ('MCH5'), 4d 43 48 31 ('MCH1'), 4d 59 20 ('MY '), which match no switch case, so `switch (Space)` falls through to the blocked default arm; the per-role overlay shows line 752 hit only by W.
why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry the literal 52 47 42 20 ('RGB ') at offset 0x10-0x13, which dispatches to `case cmsSigRgbData` (line 732, hit by both W and L) and returns TRUE before ever reaching `default:;`, so the loser is permanently stuck on the false side.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback: the equality comparisons that implement the switch on `Space` are logged by CmpLogObserver, and I2SRandReplace splices the logged comparand bytes into the input position holding the color-space signature, flipping it away from 'RGB ' to a non-recognized value that takes the default arm. Plain value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it can only nudge the FOURCC via Hamming-bucket gradient and never escapes the 'RGB ' attractor in 10 trials.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature field of the ICC profile header (input offsets 0x10-0x16, rendered 'Lab ') must spell the Lab signature so T_COLORSPACE decodes to PT_Lab and the switch in _cmsICCcolorSpace selects case PT_Lab at cmspcs.c:781; loser inputs carrying 'RGB ' / 'XYZ ' fall through to non-Lab cases.
why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds carry the literal bytes 4c 61 62 20 ('Lab ') at offsets 0x10-0x13 (and again at 0x14-0x16), so the parsed colorspace decodes to PT_Lab and the true side of the case PT_Lab branch is taken (W exclusively hits source line 781).
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed instead holds 52 47 42 20 / 58 59 5a 20 ('RGB ' / 'XYZ ') at those same offsets, so the switch never reaches PT_Lab; L takes the default arm at line 805 and leaves line 781 unhit (T=0).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the equality comparison of the parsed colorspace tag against the Lab constant, logs both operands, and splices the Lab signature bytes into the input at the matching offset; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace so they must blindly mutate a 4-byte ASCII tag (effectively a magic-value match) by chance, which never happens in 10 trials. The mechanism is identical for both decisive pairs, hence single_feature.

what_input_feature: The ICC-header data-colorspace signature field (the bytes around offset 0x10-0x13) must equal the exact 4-byte magic 'CMYK' (0x43 0x4D 0x59 0x4B), so the switch on ProfileSpace selects case cmsSigCmykData and takes the blocked true side at cmspcs.c:817.

why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offset 0x10-0x13 instead of 'CMYK'; the switch falls through every case to the default arm (cmspcs.c:872, hit only in L), so the cmsSigCmykData true side is never reached (T=0 in L).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the integer/string CMP that tests ProfileSpace against the cmsSigCmykData constant, record both operands in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the wanted 'CMYK' magic into the input where the loser's signature bytes were; naive/value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must blindly mutate to the exact 4-byte FOURCC, which they fail to do within budget.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte big-endian FOURCC at input offset 0x10 must equal the cmsSigLuvData signature 'Luv ' (4c 75 76 20). _cmsLCMScolorSpace switches on this parsed colorspace signature; only that exact value drives the switch to case cmsSigLuvData (line 819, the blocked true side).

why_loser_doesnt: All loser-blocking seeds carry 52 47 42 ('RGB') at offsets 0x10-0x12, dispatching to case cmsSigRgbData (line 815) and never reaching line 819; the loser's true-count on the blocker line is zero (T=0 F=56).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace: cmplog logs the 32-bit comparison operand cmsSigLuvData seen inside the switch and splices it back into the input at the offset that fed the compare, planting 'Luv ' exactly. value_profile (loser) lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets a CMP_MAP Hamming-distance bucket, which cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte FOURCC against an equality dispatch, so it stalls on RGB.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace-signature field at input offsets 0x10-0x13 must hold the exact magic literal 'HLS ' (bytes 48 4c 53 20) so that ProfileSpace == cmsSigHlsData selects the case at cmspcs.c:824; any other FOURCC routes to a different switch arm or the default.

mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted integer/string CMP operand for the switch dispatch, finds the seed's current 4-byte signature substring, and substitutes the logged 'HLS ' literal at that offset; naive has no CMP introspection and must blindly havoc its way onto the exact 4-byte magic, which is improbable, so naive stays blocked.

what_input_feature: The 4 bytes at offset 0x10 must equal the FOURCC 'MCH3' (4d 43 48 33), the cmsSigMCH3Data colorspace signature read by _cmsLCMScolorSpace; loser inputs hold a different signature (e.g. 'RGB ' = 52 47 42 20).

what_input_feature: The 4-byte data-colorspace FOURCC at offset 0x10-0x13 must equal the exact signature 'MCHF' (4d 43 48 46), which _cmsLCMScolorSpace compares against cmsSigMCHFData to take the true arm at line 869.

Generic mutated FOURCCs land in default:return -1, which then fails the caller's channel-count check.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature at input offset 0x10-0x13 must spell the FOURCC 'Luv ' (4c 75 76 20). The switch in cmsChannelsOfColorSpace dispatches on this value, and only cmsSigLuvData (along with the other 3-channel sister arms) reaches the blocked case at cmspcs.c:890.

mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the runtime constant 'HLS '/cmsSigHlsData (0x484c5320) was compared against the input color-space field, then substitutes that exact 4-byte operand at the matching input offset; naive has only havoc byte mutations and must blindly land all 4 magic bytes, which it never does (0/10 trials).

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace-signature field at offset 0x10 must equal the literal FOURCC 'MCH3' = 4d 43 48 33, which routes the switch in cmsChannelsOfColorSpace to the cmsSigMCH3Data case at line 897.
why_winner_satisfies: All cmplog winner seeds carry 4d(M) 43(C) 48(H) 33(3) at offsets 0x10-0x13 (12/12 in the W column), matching the cmsSigMCH3Data signature compared inside the switch.
why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser seed instead carries 52(R) 47(G) 42(B) 20( ) = 'RGB ' at offsets 0x10-0x13, which routes to the cmsSigRgbData arm and never reaches the MCH3 case.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted CMP operands (the parser comparing the read FOURCC against the 'MCH3' constant), finds the seed's FOURCC bytes, and substitutes the constant operand at offset 0x10, planting 'MCH3' that naive cannot guess; the dash-row I2S-floor signal in the winner's lineage confirms an I2S find in its ancestry.

what_input_feature: A 4-byte colorspace-signature field at input offsets 0x10-0x13 equal to the FOURCC 'LuvK' (0x4C 0x75 0x76 0x4B), which makes cmsChannelsOfColorSpace switch on ColorSpace == cmsSigLuvKData and take the true side of the case at cmspcs.c:900.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature in the ICC profile header at offsets 0x10-0x13 must hold the exact magic value 'CMYK' (43 4D 59 4B). cmsChannelsOfColorSpace switches on this signature; only cmsSigCmykData (='CMYK') reaches case line 901, the blocked true side.

why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 52 47 42 20 ('RGB ') at offsets 0x10-0x13, i.e. cmsSigRgbData, which dispatches to the 3-channel arm (line 893) and never reaches line 901; the L overlay only ever hits the default/return-3 arms.

what_input_feature: The 4 bytes at input offsets 0x10-0x13 must equal the literal FOURCC 'MCHF' (4D 43 48 46), the cmsSigMCHFData color-space signature parsed from the profile header.
why_winner_satisfies: All cmplog winner-resolving seeds carry MCHF at offset 0x10-0x13 (4D 43 48 46), and one was produced by TokenReplace placing the MCHF token verbatim.
why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser-blocking seed has 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) at offsets 0x10-0x13 instead, so the switch falls to a different/default arm and the MCHF case stays blocked.
