what_input_feature: An ICC profile whose tag-type signature/structure bytes (the 'lcms' magic prefix, profile-class FOURCC at 0x0c, color-space FOURCC at 0x10, and tag-type offset/count words) match exactly so the parser descends into Type_LUTA2B_Read, which calls _cmsReadUInt16Number(io, NULL) at line 2565 — the only call site exercising the blocked false (n==NULL) side of line 133.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry profile-class FOURCCs ('mntr','link') at offset 0x0c and color-space tokens ('RGB ','8CLR') at 0x10 and reach the deep call chain: Type_LUTA2B_Read has W hits=8 (L hits=0) and its L2565 _cmsReadUInt16Number(io,NULL) branch shows T=0 F=8 for W versus T=0 F=0 for L, so only the winner reaches the n==NULL site.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds saturate the shallow Type_Curve_Read default arm (L1171 T=57 F=3) parsing many curves but never enter Type_LUTA2B_Read (L hits=0), so the n==NULL call at line 2565 is never executed and line 133 false side stays at F=0 for the loser.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact comparand (tag-type signature / offset words) into the matching input offsets, solving the equality gates that admit deeper LUT-A2B descent; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot place the precise multi-byte FOURCC/offset values by havoc alone.

what_input_feature: A well-formed ICC profile whose header signature bytes and tag-directory offsets steer cmsReadTag to a real tag, so _cmsReadTypeBase's io->Read of sizeof(_cmsTagBase) (8 bytes) actually returns 1 record and the read condition is exercised (taking the globally-blocked true side at least once, then proceeding to return a non-zero type base). The required bytes are the profile signature/colorspace fields around offsets 0x0c-0x13 plus the 'acsp' magic region near 0x24-0x2f that gate the parser path into the tag-reading loop.
why_winner_satisfies: The vpc winner seed carries a structurally distinct profile signature region (offset 0x0c-0x13 = 'link8CLR', e.g. link device class with CLR colorspace) and 0x29=0x10, 0x2f=0x20 — byte patterns that route the parser to a tag whose type-base read succeeds. Its lineage chain contains an I2S-floor dash row, evidence the discriminating signature bytes were substituted by I2SRandReplace from logged CMP operands rather than stumbled on by havoc; the branch is reached and resolved (T=1 F=1 at L444 in the W column).
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile loser seeds all carry 'mntrRGB ' at 0x0c-0x13 and 'acspAPPL' with 0x29=0x50 / 0x2f=0x00, a header variant that never drives the parser past the gating checks to a tag whose type-base read returns 1 — at L444 the W column shows T=1 F=1 (reached and resolved) while the L column shows T=0 F=69, i.e. the loser only ever takes the false (failure) side, never resolving.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback. I2SRandReplace splices logged comparison operands (the expected profile signature / tag-type literals compared inside the lcms parser) directly into the matching input-byte positions, manufacturing the exact signature/offset bytes that let the tag-table walk reach a readable type base. value_profile has only the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix gradient and havoc/token mutators (no I2SRandReplace), so it cannot place the multi-byte literal exactly and stays blocked.

what_input_feature: The 4-byte ICC colorspace/PCS signature field (offsets ~0x10-0x16, e.g. 'MCH5'/'Lab '/'MCH1'/'XYZ ') plus the device-class/profile field (offsets 0x0c-0x0f, 'prtr' vs 'mntr') must be set to a value whose colorspace Space=cmsGetColorSpace(hInput) is NOT among the spaces _cmsEndPointsBySpace handles, so it returns FALSE and the true (blocked) branch at line 93 is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving vpc seeds carry varied, non-RGB colorspace signatures: 'MDHCXYZ', 'MCH5Lab ', 'MCH1XYZ ', 'MCH4Lab ', 'MY Lab ' at offsets 0x10+, and 'prtr' device class at 0x0c, exactly the offsets the BYTE DIFF flags as DIFFER (0x0c,0x0d,0x10,0x11,0x12). These exercise colorspaces _cmsEndPointsBySpace rejects, driving the true branch (W=10 T hits).
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking value_profile seed is locked to the same 'mntr RGB XYZ ' header ('mntr' at 0x0c, 'RGB ' at 0x10-0x13), a recognized space for which _cmsEndPointsBySpace returns TRUE, so the loser only ever takes the false branch (L T=0 F=20 at line 93) and stays blocked across all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which splices the exact 4-byte colorspace/class signature constants compared inside cmsGetColorSpace/_cmsEndPointsBySpace into the matching input-byte positions, letting vpc hit unrecognized-space signatures. Plain value_profile (havoc + token stack, no I2SRandReplace) cannot synthesize these multi-byte FOURCC literals and stays pinned at the RGB header.

what_input_feature: An ICC profile header whose 4-byte device-class / colorspace FOURCC tags (offset 0x0c onward, e.g. 'prtr', 'spac', 'scnr', or non-'mntr' colorspace combos) and the field at offset 0x3d (=0x50 'P') match the literals lcms compares against during cmsCreateTransformTHR, steering the input down a path where transform creation returns NULL (xform == NULL true side).

what_input_feature: The 4-byte device-class signature field at input offsets 0x0c-0x0f must hold one of the class FOURCCs the branch tests ('abst' = cmsSigAbstractClass, or the sibling cmsSigLinkClass/cmsSigNamedColorClass literals). what_input_feature: A PNG chunk-header whose 32-bit type field byte-equals the ASCII FOURCC 'iCCP', so the dispatcher's else-if equality test chunk_name == png_iCCP resolves true and png_handle_iCCP runs.

what_input_feature: A 4-byte chunk-name field somewhere in the PNG chunk stream that exactly equals the tRNS FOURCC, so that the equality test 'chunk_name == png_tRNS' at pngread.c:917 evaluates true and png_handle_tRNS is reached.

what_input_feature: An iTXt chunk that survives the compression-info gate at buffer[prefix_length+1] (==0 or (==1 && buffer[prefix_length+2]==PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE)) so control reaches the third loop at L2796; the translated-keyword region must contain at least one non-zero byte within `length` so the loop body executes and the false (loop-exit) side is taken at L2796.
why_winner_satisfies: cmplog reaches the L2796 loop and exits it (avg hitcount on branch winner=23, resolved 10/10); I2SRandReplace can substitute the byte values the compression-info comparison expects (buffer[prefix_length+2]==PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE) so the chunk passes the L2777-2779 gate, letting the third NUL-scan loop run to completion.
why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 with avg hitcount on branch loser=0 and stays blocked 9/10; its random havoc rarely hits the exact compression-info byte the gate compares against, so control diverts to the L2853 'bad compression info' else and never reaches L2796.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the equality CMP guarding the iTXt compression-info gate and splices the logged comparand into the input at the matching offset, manufacturing a gate-passing chunk that naive's blind havoc cannot reliably produce.

what_input_feature: An iTXt chunk where the compression-flag byte at buffer[prefix_length+1] is 0 (or 1 with a compression-type byte equal to PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE), so the chunk enters the inner block at L2779, but is NOT (compressed != 0 AND prefix_length < length) — i.e. it takes the false side of L2810 via the L2807 compressed==0 path or the trailing else 'truncated'.
why_winner_satisfies: cmplog resolves 10/10; its winning seeds carry concrete iTXt-chunk bytes (PNG magic + IHDR + gAMA + sRGB) and the byte-diff shows the compression-flag region at offset 0x2e holds fc (the chunk passes the L2779 dispatch but lands on the compressed==0 / truncated false side of the blocker at L2810).
why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 and is blocked 9/10 averaging 20.30h; the divergent-branch view shows naive (L) takes the TRUE side of L2810 (T=10 F=0) — its inputs satisfy compressed!=0 && prefix_length<length and never produce the false-side compression-flag/length combination.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer, finds the byte sequence matching one operand of the equality CMPs at L2777-2779 (compression-flag == 0/1, compression-type == PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_BASE) and substitutes the satisfying operand directly into the input, letting it pass the dispatch gate and reach the false side of the blocker that naive's blind havoc almost never hits.

what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type field (offset 0x19 in the header) must hold the exact value 0x03, the PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE constant, so that the equality test png_ptr->color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE at pngrutil.c:4457 evaluates true (also requires PNG_EXPAND set so the enclosing block at 4455 is entered).
why_winner_satisfies: All 10 cmplog winner-resolving seeds carry 0x03 at offset 0x19, and the branch true side fired in all 10 (W L4457 T=10 F=0); cmplog discovered the resolving seed at 7s.
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds never hold 0x03 at offset 0x19 (they carry 0x00 x8 and 0x04 x2), so the equality never matches and the branch stays on the false side in all 10 trials (L L4457 T=0 F=10), keeping naive blocked for 24h.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the integer CMP at pngrutil.c:4457 via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp, records both operands (the runtime color_type byte and the constant 0x03), and I2SRandReplace substitutes the constant 0x03 into the input at the offset where the current color_type byte appears; naive has only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection, so it must blindly land 0x03 on the single color_type byte.

what_input_feature: A PNG whose chunk stream includes a valid tRNS chunk (FOURCC 0x74 0x52 0x4e 0x53 = 'tRNS') that png_handle_tRNS parses, setting png_ptr->num_trans != 0, combined with an expand transformation so (png_ptr->transformations & PNG_EXPAND) != 0 — making the compound condition at L4533/L4534 true.

The winner byte sets at the chunk-FOURCC offsets 0x25-0x28 carry non-default values (e.g. 69/54/58/74) showing chunk-type bytes were mutated toward real ancillary chunks.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds never invoke png_handle_tRNS (L hits = 0), so num_trans stays zero, and the blocker L4533 evaluates false in all 10 loser trials (T=0 F=10); at the FOURCC offsets 0x25-0x28 every loser seed keeps the default 67 41 4d 41 ('gAMA') bytes with no tRNS variant.

what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at file offset 0x19 must hold the value 0x03 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) so that the equality test info_ptr->color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE is true.

why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds never hold 0x03 at offset 0x19 (L bytes 00(.)x7 04(.)x2 06(.)x1) so the color_type equality test is always false.

what_input_feature: An XML attribute whose split name resolves to the exact byte sequence name[0]='x', name[1]='m', name[2]='l', name[3]='n', name[4]='s', name[5]=0 (the literal 'xmlns' with no prefix and ns==NULL), satisfying the full six-character equality chain at SAX2.c:1170-1171.

why_loser_doesnt: value_profile only has havoc plus a CMP_MAP distance gradient; for a 6-way single-byte equality chain that terminates on a NUL, the prefix-distance bucket gives no useful gradient past the matched prefix, so the loser blocks 8/10 trials and only reaches the gate's true side once.

what_input_feature: An attribute carrying a non-NULL namespace prefix (ns != NULL) whose first six bytes do NOT spell 'x','m','l','n','s',0 — i.e. a prefixed attribute like 'x:ink' or 'xlink:...' rather than a literal 'xmlns:' namespace declaration. Taking the FALSE side of the ns[0..5]=='xmlns' gate at line 1235-1236 routes execution past line 1303 (ns != NULL search) into the winner-only block.

At line 1236 the winner takes the FALSE side (W(T/F): T=0 F=6) and then enters the winner-only branch where xmlSearchNs / the undefined-namespace-prefix error path runs (lines 1306-1309 marked [W]).

mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer logged by the byte-wise comparisons against the 'xmlns' literal (ns[0]=='x' ... ns[5]==0) and substitutes the captured operand bytes into the input at the matching offsets, letting cmplog construct an attribute prefix that is byte-distinct from 'xmlns' yet structurally a valid prefixed attribute — solving the multi-byte string roadblock that naive's blind havoc rarely lands on.

what_input_feature: The input must carry an attribute fullname byte-equal to the 6-byte literal "xml:id" so that xmlStrEqual(fullname, BAD_CAST "xml:id") returns true at SAX2.c:1415.

what_input_feature: The first three bytes of the input must equal the literal magic sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF (the UTF-8 byte-order mark) so that the conjunctive equality gate in[0]==0xEF && in[1]==0xBB && in[2]==0xBF evaluates true.

what_input_feature: An XML entity reference whose parsed name begins with the byte 'l' (0x6c), reaching `case 'l':` in the switch on name[0], ideally spelling the predefined entity name 'lt'. The byte that selects the case is the first character of the entity name string passed to xmlGetPredefinedEntity.

mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog run I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the switch/xmlStrEqual comparison on the entity name and splices the logged comparand ('l' / 'lt') into the input bytes at the matching offset; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the exact name byte by chance, so they stall at default. value_profile's distance-bucket comparison feedback does not help here because a string-equality entity-name match is a discrete switch-case hit rather than a smooth distance signal — confirmed by value_profile losing identically to naive.

what_input_feature: A parsed entity-reference name (passed as `name` to xmlGetPredefinedEntity) whose first byte name[0] equals 'a' (0x61), so the switch(name[0]) selects `case 'a':`; concretely the predefined entity names & or ' reaching the function via xmlSAX2GetEntity.
why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner repeatedly reaches and resolves the 'a'-case: it takes the true side 63 times (W T=63 F=0 at line 307) and the enclosing function shows hitcount 130 on the branch for the winner, indicating the entity-name dispatch lands on 'a' and exercises the amp/apos xmlStrEqual comparisons (lines 308/310 hit only on the W side).
why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser never selects `case 'a':` (L T=0 F=479 at line 307); every one of its 479 invocations falls through to `default:` (L T=479 F=0 at line 317), so name[0] is never 'a' and the amp/apos comparison lines 308/310 are never reached (T=0 F=0 for the loser).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer, which captures the operands of the switch dispatch on name[0] and the xmlStrEqual(name, "amp"/"apos") string comparisons, then substitutes the logged constant operand ('a', "amp", "apos") into the input bytes at the offsets where the current name appears, manufacturing entity names that route through `case 'a':`; naive has only blind havoc/token mutation and no CMP introspection, so it must stumble onto the exact first byte by chance and almost never does.

what_input_feature: An XML document that registers at least two distinct named entries (name/name2/name3 triples) in xmlHashAddEntry3 whose computed key collides into the same bucket, so the bucket already holds a linked chain (table->table[key] has a non-NULL ->next) when a later entry is added.
why_winner_satisfies: cmplog resolves the branch in 8/10 trials with avg hitcount 1 on the branch; its I2SRandReplace mutator substitutes logged comparison operands from the chain-walk equality checks (insert->name == name) back into input bytes, producing several distinct-yet-related names that accumulate in one bucket and grow the ->next chain so the for-loop guard's true side is taken.
why_loser_doesnt: naive resolves only 1/10 trials with avg hitcount 0; its havoc/token mutators flip bytes blindly and tend to produce a single distinct name or no collision, so the bucket stays empty (insert == NULL via valid == 0) or holds exactly one entry with ->next == NULL, leaving the true branch untaken.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recorded at the name-equality comparisons in the chain-walk and splices a matched operand into the input at the offset where it appeared, generating extra hash entries that share a bucket; this comparison-introspection capability is exactly what naive lacks, explaining the cmplog>naive divergence.

what_input_feature: A well-formed DOCTYPE/DTD payload that parses far enough to insert a genuinely new (name,name2,name3) hash triple via xmlHashAddEntry3 on the dict path (line 571 true), so line 580 takes the false (non-duplicate) side. The structural literals (DOCTYPE, SYSTEM, element/entity names) must survive intact through the parser.

why_loser_doesnt: Naive resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked 8/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch; without I2S substitution it relies on random havoc to keep the multi-byte structural tokens that gate deeper parsing, so the parse aborts before a distinct triple is inserted.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand into input offsets matching the other, repairing the byte literals that gate deeper DTD parsing; the lineage of the winning seed shows I2S-floor dash rows confirming an I2S contribution.

what_input_feature: The input must place the literal name3 byte sequence (e.g. an entity/element name from the DTD) at the offset the parser interns, so that after dict interning the stored entry->name3 and the queried name3 are the same pointer.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog runs I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CMP buffer, finds where one comparison operand appears in the input, and substitutes the matching operand byte-for-byte, directly placing the required name3 literal (resolved 8/10).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator, so it cannot copy the exact operand into the input and must rely on havoc luck, staying blocked 8/10.
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (present in vpc, absent in value_profile) substitutes the logged CMP operand into the input to satisfy the name comparison feeding the dict-interned name3 identity; this I2S delta is the only difference between the two fuzzers.

what_input_feature: The same gate: a <!-- comment opener (the '-' = 0x2D comparand) plus a literal 0x09 tab byte in the comment body satisfying *in == 0x09.

why_loser_doesnt: naive has no CMP interception and no I2SRandReplace, so it must stumble onto the exact 0x09 byte inside a valid comment by blind havoc; its blocking seeds never form a comment body carrying a tab and stay on the False side (cmplog resolved 8/10, naive only 2/10).
mechanism_attribution: input-to-state substitution: I2SRandReplace splices the logged comparand byte (0x09 / 0x2D) into the matching input offset, supplying the byte-exact tab the comment-content loop tests for.

what_input_feature: Inside a well-formed DTD <!ELEMENT ...> children content declaration, after the parser consumes an element Name (the 'else' branch at line 6347), the very next input byte at the parse cursor (RAW) must be '?' (0x3f) so the equality test `if (RAW == '?')` at parser.c:6358 takes its true branch.

why_loser_doesnt: value_profile resolves only 2/10 and never takes the blocker true side (W T=6 vs L T=0 at L6358); its inputs reach the surrounding parse machinery but the byte at the cursor after the element Name is not '?', so the equality comparison only ever yields false, draining time into the *,+,else arms (L6361 T=21, L6367) instead.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique distinguishing winner (value_profile_cmplog) from loser (value_profile) is I2SRandReplace: both carry value-profile distance feedback, but only vpc has the cmplog CMP buffer + I2SRandReplace mutator that logs the '?' operand of `RAW == '?'` and substitutes it directly into the input cursor, deterministically satisfying the single-byte equality gate that distance-bucket feedback alone does not flip.

what_input_feature: A '<!' followed by a '-' byte at the NXT(2) position, so the switch on NXT(2) selects case '-' and calls xmlParseComment. The single discriminating byte is the '-' (0x2d) immediately after '<!'.

why_loser_doesnt: naive has only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection; its corpus seeds carry '<!DOCTYPE'/SYSTEM markup (case 'E'/'A'/'N' arms) and it never lands the exact '-' byte at NXT(2) within budget, so case '-' stays at T=0 for all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace substitutes the logged switch operand '-' at the NXT(2) byte offset, which plain naive havoc cannot reliably hit; this is the input-to-state comparison-solving technique distinguishing cmplog from naive.

what_input_feature: Byte-exact XML structural tokens (the '<?xml version="1.0"?>' prolog and '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/...' markup) must appear unbroken at the document head so the parser keeps wellFormed==1 long enough to parse an entity reference whose name resolves to no declared entity (ent==NULL).

mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads intercepted CMP operands (the literal prolog/markup keyword comparisons) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected literal at the matching input offsets, repairing the structural tokens that naive havoc can only stumble onto by luck.

what_input_feature: A well-formed XML document with a DOCTYPE/DTD that declares an attribute defaulting an unprefixed 'xmlns' (default-namespace) attribute via ATTLIST, so the defaulting loop at L9626 produces an entry whose attname == ctxt->str_xmlns and aprefix == NULL, taking the true branch at L9633.

why_loser_doesnt: The loser seeds never reach the true branch: one loser carries a structurally similar prolog+DOCTYPE but the run produces only the false side / earlier arms (L T=0 F=8 at L9633, and the aprefix==str_xmlns arm L9648 fires T=5 F=3 instead), while the other loser is malformed nested '<a>'/'<b>' tag soup with no DTD defaulting at all, so xmlParseStartTag2 stalls in the attribute scan and never builds an xmlns-default entry.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback. The intervening literal comparisons in the prolog/DTD ('<?xml', 'version', DOCTYPE keyword, the str_xmlns dictionary pointer compare) are exact-equality gates; I2SRandReplace splices the logged comparand bytes directly into the input so the document parses far enough to populate attsDefault and reach the L9633 xmlns-default true branch. value_profile (loser) lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets prefix-distance gradient buckets, which do not synthesize the exact byte sequences needed to clear the structural gates.

what_input_feature: An input where the byte at the parse cursor (after optional 4-byte encoding detection) is 0x00, satisfying the integer comparison CUR == 0 at parser.c:10852.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry NUL bytes early in the buffer (e.g. seed 1 has 5c 0a 00 00 00 around offset 0x04-0x07 and seed 2 has 0a 00 80 at offset 0x05-0x07); the cmplog winner resolved the branch in 8/10 trials, taking the W-marked true-branch at lines 10853-10855.

what_input_feature: CDATA-scanned content bytes that include one of the three exact control codepoints 0x0A, 0x0D, or 0x09 at a position where xmlCheckCdataPush evaluates a sub-0x20 1-byte char; the byte must equal one of those three values, otherwise the else arm at L11353 returns -ix and the blocker true side at L11351 is never taken.

mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of value_profile: the cmplog CMP buffer records the literal comparands 0x0A/0x0D/0x09 from the equality CMPs at L11351, and I2SRandReplace splices one of those logged bytes directly into the input at the offset feeding c, deterministically satisfying the equality that value_profile's distance-bucket feedback plus havoc cannot land on a single exact byte.

what_input_feature: The byte at the parser cursor when it reaches XML_PARSER_START with avail>=2 (i.e. the first content byte after the 4-byte encoding-detection prefix is consumed) must be 0x00, so that the comparison `cur == 0` at parser.c:11539 evaluates true and takes the document-empty/halt path.
why_winner_satisfies: cmplog winner seeds carry embedded NUL bytes near the cursor region (e.g. Seed 3 has 00 at offset 0x2b, Seed 4 has 00 at offset 0x28), and on the branch the winner takes the true side (W L11539 T=8 F=0) reaching lines 11540-11551 marked [W]. cmplog's I2SRandReplace can read the intercepted `cur == 0` operand and splice a 0x00 byte at the matching input offset.
why_loser_doesnt: naive never resolves (0/10, blocked=10/10); at the branch it takes only the false side (L L11539 T=0 F=73), so its cursor byte is non-zero at the moment the START case is evaluated. naive seeds contain NUL bytes only at non-cursor offsets (e.g. Seed 1 at 0x02-0x03, Seed 3 at 0x00-0x02) that get consumed or shifted out of the decisive cursor position; naive has no CMP introspection to deliberately place 0x00 there.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the single-byte comparison `cur == 0` via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp and records operand 0 in the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then substitutes 0x00 into the input at the offset feeding the cursor, deterministically driving the true branch. naive can only reach 0x00 by blind havoc, which is improbable at the exact cursor position.

what_input_feature: Input bytes that, after DTD parsing, hand xmlSplitQName2 a name whose first character is the colon ':' (0x3a) so the equality test name[0] == ':' takes the true branch; the colon must land at the start of an element/attribute QName, not anywhere arbitrary.

why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seed is almost entirely zero bytes (offsets 0x06,0x07,0x09-0x12,0x24-0x31 all 0x00 where the winner has ASCII), so it does not decode to a GET/HEAD method; it reaches use_range true (L2852 T=1) but fails the GET/HEAD test, taking F at 2858 into the POST/PUT else-if at 2865 ([L] lines).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer (recording the operands of the httpreq==HTTPREQ_GET/HEAD integer compares and the strcmp/memcmp of method/header tokens) and substitutes the matching constant operand into the input at the compared offset, manufacturing the exact method/token bytes that naive can only reach by blind havoc.

what_input_feature: Bytes forming a structurally valid URL: a scheme/`://` separator, a numeric port, and an HTTP version token, so curl actually creates and caches a connection that is later torn down via Curl_conncache_close_all_connections, which calls Curl_conncache_remove_conn with lock=TRUE — taking the true side of `if(lock)`.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry a literal `://` separator at offsets 0x0a-0x0c, a `:9001` port followed by `/850`, and a `HTTP/27.0` version token at 0x24-0x2c, i.e. a parseable connection target whose teardown reaches the locked-remove path (the W-only CONNCACHE_LOCK lines 284/295 are hit).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short (35-73 bytes) with `://` replaced by run-length `%`/`.`/`+` filler and no port or HTTP-version token, so no real connection is cached and the false (lock=0) side dominates (L hits L283 F=20, never the true side).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact comparison operands (scheme/`://`, port `9001`, `HTTP/` token) that the URL parser compares against, building the structured connection target by construction; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must stumble onto these multi-byte literals via blind havoc, which they fail to do within budget.

what_input_feature: Input bytes forming a recognizable URL/scheme + HTTP-version token (e.g. 'po%' scheme prefix at 0x06, '://' separator, and the 'HTTP/' literal at offset 0x24) so curl actually parses a connection and inserts a connectbundle into connc->hash; only then is he non-NULL and while(he) takes the true branch.
why_winner_satisfies: Winning seeds carry a stable structured prefix: 'po' (70 6f) at 0x06-0x07, '://' and digits forming a host:port, and the 'HTTP/27.0' literal (47 48 54 54 50 2f at 0x24) which is concentrated across winners; this drives connection-cache population so Curl_conncache_size and close_connect_only fire only under W.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (35-73 bytes) and lack the coherent scheme/host/HTTP-literal structure (offsets fill with '%','+','A','.' filler), so no connection is cached, the hash is empty, he is NULL, and while(he) stays false.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact comparison operands (scheme/HTTP-version literals) into the input at matching offsets, synthesizing the structured tokens that drive cache population; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must stumble on these multi-byte literals by chance.

what_input_feature: A header buffer beginning with the literal protocol-status prefix "HTTP/" (bytes 47 48 54 54 50 2f around offset 0x24) so that checkprotoprefix returns non-STATUS_BAD, a full header line is consumed, k->headerline is incremented past 0, and the blocker's false (else-of-first-header) side is taken instead of the perpetually-true !k->headerline guard.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the exact ASCII run "HTTP/" at offset 0x24 (e.g. "GHTTP/" 47 48 54 54 50 2f), so checkprotoprefix/sscanf match the protocol line and the status-line parse path (Curl_http_statusline, verify_header) executes only in W.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds have no "HTTP/" literal at the protocol-prefix offsets; their bytes there are scattered (0x24 = 50/42/9e/2e, 0x25 = 25/2e/41/64, etc.), so checkprotoprefix never recognizes a header line, k->headerline stays 0, and only the true side of the blocker is ever taken (loser hitcount on branch = 0).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recorded when sscanf/checkprotoprefix compares the input against the constant "HTTP/" token and substitutes that exact operand into the input, planting the literal that naive/value_profile can only reach by blind havoc. value_profile only rewards distance-bucket closeness via feedback and has no operand-substitution mutator, so vp cannot plant the exact literal and also loses.

what_input_feature: A header payload whose first complete header line begins with the literal protocol token 'HTTP/' (bytes 0x47 0x54 0x54 0x50 0x2f) at the start of the status line, so checkprotoprefix/sscanf recognizes it as a valid first header and the false (already-counted) side of `if(!k->headerline)` is reached on the next line.
why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'HTTP/' bytes (offsets 0x24-0x29 hold 47(G)/48(H)/54(T)/54(T)/50(P)/2f(/)), which sscanf parses as a status line so k->headerline is incremented and the false branch is taken; winners resolve 10/10 with hitcount ~4739-4903.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 'From:' (offsets 0x26-0x2e show 72(r)/6f(o)/6d(m)/3a(:)/73(s)) instead of the protocol token, so checkprotoprefix returns STATUS_BAD and the function returns CURLE_UNSUPPORTED_PROTOCOL before the false branch; losers block 10/10 with hitcount 0.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operand of the strcmp/memcmp inside checkprotoprefix (the 'HTTP/' literal) and substitutes it into the input at the matching offset, synthesizing the protocol prefix that pure havoc (naive/value_profile) cannot land on the multi-byte literal; both decisive pairs differ only by the I2S stage.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid URL: leading length/type header bytes followed by a recognizable scheme and a host:port authority (e.g. a 'po...://...:9001/...' form) plus an 'HTTP/...' protocol token, so curl actually parses, connects, and stores a connectdata in connc->hash. Only then is he non-NULL at conncache.c:369 and the true (blocked) side taken.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry a coherent connect-able structure: bytes at the URL offsets are concentrated runs spelling a scheme/host/port ('po%.://12%/0((.1:9001/850') and a 'GHTTP/27.0' token, and the branch is hit 332 (vpc) / 45 (cmplog) times — a real connection was cached and the close-all loop iterated it.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (32-36 byte) garbled runs of '+++++', '%%%%', 'AAAA' etc. that never form a valid URL authority; no connection is established, connc->hash stays empty, and he is NULL so only the false branch (return NULL) is taken (hitcount 13 / 1).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets, solving the scheme/protocol comparison checkpoints in curl's URL parser so a connectable URL is synthesized; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit those literal comparisons by chance, so they rarely build a cached connection.

what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed URL with a recognizable scheme/host:port (e.g. literal bytes like '://1','HTTP/' and a port '9001') so curl actually opens and caches a connection; only then is conncache_find_first_connection non-NULL and the while(conn) true-side taken during close-all.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry concentrated structural tokens at fixed offsets: '://' and '9001' (0x0a..0x1e) and the 'HTTP/2' / 'GHTTP/27.0' magic at 0x24+, which the cmplog/vpc I2S stage planted by substituting CMP operands; the branch's true side is hit 332 (vpc) / 45 (cmplog) times.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short, scheme-corrupted byte runs (e.g. '%' / '+' / 'A' fill) lacking the '://' + port + HTTP-version structure at the same offsets, so no connection is cached and the loop body never runs (hitcount 13 / 1).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace, which reads CMP operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the matching operand bytes (scheme/port/HTTP-version literals) into the input; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot place these multi-byte literals to make a connection get cached.

what_input_feature: A byte in the escaped string (part name/filename) that equals one of the formtable first characters: '"' (0x22), '\r' (0x0D), or '\n' (0x0A). Matching such a byte makes the inner loop find *p, leading the escape branch to be taken and the blocked false-exit of the table scan to be reached with a match.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving vpc seed carries 0d (carriage return, a formtable key) at offset 0x20, and the winner reaches the escape path Curl_dyn_add(&db, *p + 1) at line 325 which is marked [W] (winner-only); on the blocker line 321 the winner takes the false side (F=2).
why_loser_doesnt: The loser value_profile seeds are dominated by printable ASCII runs ('reee'/'rrrr'/'4444') with no 0x22/0x0D/0x0A escape characters, so the inner table scan always exhausts to *p==NULL and the escape branch (line 325) is never taken.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP; the byte comparison **p != *src is intercepted by cmplog, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the constant table operand (0x22/0x0D/0x0A) directly into the input position holding *src, planting the escape-trigger byte that value_profile's distance-bucket feedback cannot synthesize on its own.

what_input_feature: A header block containing the literal ASCII bytes "Content-Type" (43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 2d 54 79 70 65) so that search_header's match_header finds a matching header, sets value non-NULL, and the loop condition !value becomes false on the next iteration — taking the blocked false side at mime.c:350.
why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds carry the contiguous ASCII run "Content-Typ" at offsets 0x24-0x2f (47 43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 2d 54 79 70 = G C o n t e n t - T y p), the exact case-sensitive prefix curl searches for via STRCONST("Content-Type").
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry no "Content-Type" run; at the offsets where winners hold the literal (0x25-0x2c) the loser bytes diverge entirely (e.g. 80, 2e, fe, eb, ff), so match_header never matches, value stays NULL, and the loop only ever takes its true side.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the string CMP inside match_header (__sanitizer_weak_hook_strcmp/memcmp), record both operands, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the program-side constant "Content-Type" into the input at matching offsets. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the 12-byte case-sensitive literal by chance, which they never do across 10 trials.

what_input_feature: The input must embed the literal ASCII string 'Content-Type:' (and an enclosing MIME/userheaders structure) so that search_header(part->userheaders, STRCONST("Content-Type")) returns non-NULL, making customct non-NULL at line 1792 and taking the true branch (contenttype = customct).
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the exact byte run 'Content-Type:' starting at offset 0x24 (47 43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 2d 54 79 70 65 3a = 'GContent-Type:'); the per-offset byte-set shows W concentrated on these literal bytes (e.g. 0x24=47('G'), 0x25=43('C'), 0x26=6f('o') with x20 counts), matching the STRCONST("Content-Type") operand the comparison interception logs.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds lack the 'Content-Type:' literal at those offsets (e.g. naive seeds carry runs of 2d('-') and 00 bytes; offset 0x25 L bytes are 80/00/41/74 not 43('C')), so search_header finds no matching header, customct stays NULL, and the false branch is taken (L1792 T=0 F=41).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer recording the STRCONST("Content-Type") string-CMP operand and substitutes that literal into the input at matching offsets, synthesizing the header string that naive/value_profile can only stumble on by chance; value_profile_cmplog wins over value_profile by the same I2S mutator (the only delta between vp and vpc is I2SRandReplace).

what_input_feature: The fuzz input must build a MIME part whose Content-Type is set to a concrete string (a custom 'Content-Type:' header value) so that contenttype is non-NULL when reaching line 1828; the false side of the branch then requires that contenttype be compared by strncasecompare against the 10-byte literal "multipart/". Bytes must spell out a recognizable 'Content-Type:' header token followed by a value.
why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving (value_profile_cmplog) seeds embed the ASCII run 'Content-Typ'/'Content-Type:' at offsets 0x24-0x37 followed by a value such as 'where'/'miltip', so contenttype is set and the strncasecompare(contenttype,"multipart/",10) call executes and resolves the false branch (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1828 shows W T=10 F=11 / W T=10 F=0 — winner takes both sides).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds carry corrupted/garbage payloads ('reee/rrrzrr', 'sslv23', random b7/ff bytes) at the offsets where the winner spells 'Content-Type:', so the parser never assembles a usable contenttype reaching the strncasecompare; DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1828 shows L T=10 F=0 (loser only ever takes the true side, never the blocked false side).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that value_profile lacks. strncasecompare(contenttype,"multipart/",10) is a string comparison whose constant operand "multipart/" is captured in the CmpLog buffer; I2SRandReplace substitutes that captured constant (and the surrounding 'Content-Type:' tokens captured at other CMPs) directly into the input at the operand offset, planting the exact bytes the parser needs in one step. value_profile has no I2SRandReplace and can only nudge bytes by coarse distance-bucket feedback, which never assembles the multi-byte header token, so it stays blocked.

what_input_feature: A parsed mime/header configuration whose disposition string equals the exact literal "attachment" (10 bytes, case-insensitive), so that curl_strequal(disposition, "attachment") returns true and the true branch at mime.c:1830 is taken; the winning corpus reaches it via a structured Content-Type header section that lets disposition be set and compared against the embedded literal.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the structured header bytes 'Content-Typ' at offsets 0x24+ and a populated header region (e.g. 'Content-Type:where'); the blocker true side is reached 28 times by W and 0 by L, and W spends far less time blocked (cmplog 6.20h vs naive 22.50h). I2S in cmplog/vpc copies the 'attachment' operand recorded by the strcmp/strequal CMP callback directly into the disposition bytes, manufacturing the exact match havoc almost never stumbles onto.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds (naive/value_profile) are short 42/38/44-byte inputs whose header region is absent or garbage (e.g. '------d', '%A&%%c%dC', 'reee/rrr'); they never present a disposition equal to "attachment", so curl_strequal returns false (L hits the true side 0 times, false side 21) and the branch stays blocked for ~21-22h.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace: the curl_strequal/strcmp callback records the literal operand "attachment", and I2SRandReplace substitutes it into the input at the disposition offset, satisfying the exact-string equality. naive and plain value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must blindly mutate the multi-byte literal and fail.

what_input_feature: A Content-Type header value reaching content_type_match must be a string that differs from the exact literal "multipart/form-data" so the strcmp-style comparison fails and the false branch is taken; winners carry a parsed Content-Type field (e.g. "Content-Type:where") that is non-empty but is NOT the magic literal.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds embed an explicit "Content-Type:" header byte run at offsets 0x24-0x2b (47 43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 = 'GContent', then 'Type:where'), so cthdr is set, the loop at L2381 advances past it, contenttype is non-null yet not equal to multipart/form-data, and content_type_match returns false (the blocked F side). The I2S substitution stage planted the comparison-relevant string content that distinguishes this branch.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds lack the parsed Content-Type field: at L2380 if(cthdr) is False (T=0 F=20), so the else-if at L2383 sets cthdr = "multipart/form-data" verbatim, and at the blocker L1897 content_type_match is True (T=20 F=0) — the loser is pinned to the magic literal and can only ever take the True side.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer from the content_type_match comparison and substitutes operand bytes, manufacturing a Content-Type value that satisfies the if(cthdr) path while differing from the multipart/form-data literal; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and never construct the required header byte sequence within budget.

what_input_feature: A '%d'-class (FORMAT_INT, non-CHAR, non-OCTAL, non-HEX, non-UNSIGNED) decimal conversion whose argument value, parsed from the harness input, is < 0 so that p->data.num.as_signed < 0 evaluates true at line 735.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry a structured header (00 01 00 00 00 19 ...) plus '%'-format bytes that drive an INT conversion with a negative operand; the per-role overlay marks the negative-handling block (lines 738-741, 781, 788) as winner-only [W], and the DIVERGENT BRANCHES line 735 shows W=T:5 F:20 while L never takes the true side (T:0).
why_loser_doesnt: Naive loser seeds reach line 735 (the case-FORMAT_INT path is hit, L F:40) but every integer conversion they construct is non-negative, so is_neg stays 0 and the true branch is never taken; naive must blindly mutate bytes into a value whose signed interpretation is negative.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer, where the as_signed<0 comparison and the upstream format/parameter CMPs are recorded, and substitutes operand bytes into the input so it constructs a negative-valued %d conversion; naive lacks any CMP introspection and only stumbles onto a negative value by chance, which it managed in only 1/10 trials.

what_input_feature: The fuzz input must form a format directive routed to FORMAT_INT decimal conversion (base 10) whose signed value is negative, so is_neg becomes 1 and the sign-prefix guard at line 780 takes its true side; this requires a coherent %d/%i spec with an argument byte sequence decoding to a negative integer.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry a structured header/URL-like prefix (e.g. bytes 00 01 00 00 00 19 then a 'WS'/'po' tag, ':/', '127.0.0.1:9001', '850') that drives the parser into the decimal-integer arm; the W-only overlay shows lines 738-741 (negative-to-positive conversion) and 781 (--width) hit, and the DIVERGENT BRANCHES table shows line 735/736 reaching is_neg true (T=5) and line 787 OUTCHAR('-') reached only under W.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (34-36 bytes) and dominated by literal '%'/'A'/'+' bytes (e.g. %`%a, %A, %C@%, %%Cb%ca) that route into the FLAGS_CHAR ('%c') arm and string formatting rather than a base-10 integer with a negative value; the L overlay never reaches lines 738-741 or 781, and line 736 is_neg shows T=0 F=40 for L.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes intercepted CMP operands into the input at matching offsets, letting it construct the precise parser-routing bytes (tag/colon/path/numeric fields) that produce a negative decimal integer; naive lacks CMP interception and must hit this multi-byte structural condition by blind havoc, which it does only 1/10 trials.

what_input_feature: A structured request whose leading bytes spell a recognizable scheme/host/port and HTTP token (e.g. the 'pop3'/'9001'/'HTTP/' literals at offsets 0x06-0x2b) so the transfer reaches multi_done with result==0 and a nonzero Curl_pgrsDone rc, taking the true branch at multi.c:654.
why_winner_satisfies: vpc winning seeds carry concentrated structural literals — '/:::' run at 0x0b-0x10, '9001' at 0x17-0x1a, and the 'GHTTP/ m' token at 0x24-0x2b — that the cmplog I2S stage substituted to satisfy the scheme/header comparisons, driving execution down to the blocker with !result true and rc nonzero (winner avg hitcount on branch 1674).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds are short, '%'-padded noise (e.g. 'CdUC%%%%!%e', './/././') lacking the matching scheme/port/HTTP literals; they never reach a state where multi_done sees result==0 with a nonzero rc, so they stay on the false side (loser avg hitcount 1).
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share value_profile's comparison-distance feedback; the only technique delta is vpc's I2SRandReplace, which copies the parser's expected comparison operands directly into the input, manufacturing the exact scheme/port/HTTP-token bytes that the distance feedback alone could not climb to within budget.

what_input_feature: A structured request input that parses into a valid scheme://host:port/path URL (e.g. the bytes 70 6f 25 ... 3a 2f 2f '://' host '12' ':9001/850' and a 'GHTTP/27.0.' line with a long '////' path) so the resulting connection is cleanly completed and NOT marked for close, driving the multi_done branch '|| conn->bits.close' to FALSE and taking the connection-reuse else arm at line 718.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving cmplog seeds carry a coherent URL skeleton: offset 0x06-0x0f spells 'po%.://12%/0((.1:9001/850\0' and offset 0x24 onward is 'GHTTP/27.0.' followed by a long '/' run, so the request reaches multi_done with conn->bits.close FALSE (L711 W=T:0/F:17, the false side) and proceeds into the else reuse block (lines 718-742 marked [W]).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (35 bytes) and byte-noise dominated by '%','+','A' fills (e.g. seed 2 '%`%a>f..c%+]%A.AAAA@AAZpermit'), so they never build a reusable connection; they always take the true disconnect arm (L711 L=T:20/F:0) executing connclose/Curl_disconnect at lines 713-716 marked [L].
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc add I2SRandReplace, which reads intercepted CMP operands (URL-scheme, host, port, HTTP-version literal comparisons in curl's parser) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected operand into the input at matching offsets, synthesizing the structured URL bytes that naive/value_profile cannot assemble by blind havoc; the winning cmplog seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows confirming an I2S contribution.

what_input_feature: Input bytes forming a parseable URL: a recognized scheme literal (e.g. 'pop3') at offset 0x06 followed by host and a numeric port (e.g. '9001'/'900') so the state machine advances data->mstate past MSTATE_INIT into a CONNECT/PROTOCONNECT state (< MSTATE_COMPLETED) when curl_multi_remove_handle is reached, making premature TRUE.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry a structured 'pop3' scheme followed by a host string and ':900'/'9001' port and HTTP-like headers; the per-role overlay shows W taking the true side at L790 (T=4 F=0) and entering premature/num_alive--/multi_done at 794/797/814, confirming a connected non-COMPLETED state at removal.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short (32-36 bytes) runs of '+'/'%'/'.' noise with no valid scheme byte at 0x06 and no host:port, so the handle never leaves the early INIT/CONNECT failure path; the overlay shows L only ever taking the false side at L790 (T=0 F=20), never setting premature TRUE.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer for the scheme- and port-comparison operands curl performs during URL parsing and substitutes the matching literals into the input at the compared offsets, letting the parser accept the scheme/host/port and advance the multi state machine; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit the exact scheme/port bytes by chance.

what_input_feature: A length-prefixed pop3 URL whose host/port/path bytes parse into a connectable scheme, driving the easy handle past MSTATE_DO into an active mid-transfer state with data->conn non-NULL when curl_multi_remove_handle runs (line 800 true branch).
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry a clean structured 'pop3' scheme followed by a concentrated host/port literal (':::::::::900', '0.1:9001?850') and a HTTP/-like header block; the connection advances so data->conn is set and mstate is between MSTATE_DO and MSTATE_COMPLETED, and per-role coverage marks the true-branch body (lines 805/814) [W].
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds after the 'pop3'-region bytes degenerate into long runs of '%'/'+'/'.' filler (e.g. 'pk+++++*++' and '%%%%%%%%%%%%') that do not parse into a valid host:port, so the handle never reaches an active connection and the line-800 condition stays false (L hits the false-path msglist loop at lines 891-898).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads CMP operands captured during URL/scheme parsing and substitutes the exact byte sequences (scheme token, port digits, separators) into the input, building the precise host:port string that naive/value_profile cannot assemble by blind havoc; the I2S-floor dash row in the winning seed's lineage confirms an I2S contribution.

what_input_feature: A byte string parseable as a valid scheme://host:port URL — the winner seeds carry a literal 'pop3' scheme token followed by a host and a ':900' port and a digit run, structured enough to drive the easy handle through the multi_runsingle state machine down to curl_multi_remove_handle, where the message-queue loop at line 891 exits immediately (false side) because no pending message targets this handle.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds contain the literal scheme bytes 'pop3' at offset 0x06 and a ':900' / port-like digit run (':::::::900' / ':9001') in the 0x10-0x1f region, plus an embedded 'HTTP/' request line — the structured URL parses and connects, so execution reaches line 891 and takes the false (loop-exit) side; the per-role overlay marks line 891 [W] resolving and the cmplog winner shows hit-count 2 on the branch.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short, repetitive byte runs ('pk+++++*++', '%`%a>f', long '%%%%' / 'AAAA' fills) with no coherent scheme://host:port structure; they fail to parse/connect, so they never reach line 891 with an empty msglist and instead stall earlier in the state machine (loser blocked the branch in 8/10 trials).
mechanism_attribution: cmplog / value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace, which reads intercepted CMP operands (scheme-string compares, port-integer compares along the URL parse and connect path) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the expected operand into the input, synthesizing the structured 'pop3://...:900' URL that naive/value_profile cannot stumble onto by havoc; this lets only the I2S-equipped arm drive execution deep enough to reach and exit the message-queue loop.

what_input_feature: A structured input whose leading bytes form a parseable URL with a scheme separator '://' (offsets ~0x0a-0x0c) and a numeric port like '9001' (offsets ~0x19-0x1c) followed by a protocol token 'HTTP/' / 'GHTTP/27.0' near offset 0x24, driving curl's connect/FTP state machine deep enough that Curl_closesocket -> Curl_multi_closed runs while data->multi is NULL (false side of if(multi)).
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are 131-byte structured inputs containing '://', port digits '9001', and 'GHTTP/27.0' literals; their per-offset byte sets are concentrated on these protocol characters (e.g. '/'x14 at 0x0c, '9'x15 at 0x17, '0'x14 at 0x19) and they take the false branch, with winner avg hitcount 332 vs loser 13.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short 35-byte low-structure inputs dominated by '%','+','A' fill bytes with no coherent '://' / port / 'HTTP/' literals; they take the true branch and the state machine never reaches the socket-close path with multi==NULL.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads intercepted string/integer CMPs in curl's URL and protocol parser and substitutes the exact expected literals ('://', port number, 'HTTP/') into the input, letting it pass the comparison gates that advance the connect/FTP state machine; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot synthesize these multi-byte literals by chance.

why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short and byte-noise: at offset 0x24 W is uniformly 'G' but L is a scattered set (50/42/9e/2f), and the entire "HTTP/".."Set-Coo" region is dominated by '%' filler in L, so the response never parses a Set-Cookie header; Curl_cookie_add never runs and the freelist while(co) stays on the F side (T=0).

what_input_feature: The input must drive curl through the cookie-parsing path so that at least one cookie is successfully added (c->numcookies > 0 at cookie_output time). This requires structured magic-byte prefix bytes plus a literal 'HTTP/' response token and a literal 'Set-Cookie' header with a parseable name=value and domain, so the false branch (numcookies==0) is escaped.

why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds at the same offsets carry scattered, unconcentrated bytes (mostly '%','A','+','.' filler) instead of the 'HTTP/' / 'Set-Cookie' literals; Curl_cookie_add and get_netscape_format never execute (L hits = 0), so no cookie is stored and line 1690 stays on the false branch in all 10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog carry I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the exact compared operand (e.g. the 'HTTP/' / cookie-header literals) into the input at matching offsets, solving the literal-byte gates that gate cookie parsing. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace; value_profile's Hamming-closeness feedback only rewards approaching operands and cannot synthesize the exact multi-byte literals needed, so both losers stall.

mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets, letting it plant the literal scheme/authority and field bytes that curl's parser compares against; naive only has havoc/token mutators with no CMP introspection, so it rarely synthesizes the structured prefix needed to reach the guard's T side.

what_input_feature: Bytes that parse into a well-formed URL/protocol so a connection bundle is added to the connection-cache hash; reaching the true branch requires the iterator to already hold a current_element (i.e. a populated, multi-node hash list traversed past its first slot). The winner inputs carry a valid scheme/authority structure (`://`, host digits, port `9001`, `HTTP/2` token) that survives parsing.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds are 131 bytes with structured fields: a `://` separator, host `12`, port `9001`, an `HTTP/27.0` version token, and a long run of `/` path bytes — enough valid structure for curl to create and cache a connection so the iterator advances and current_element becomes non-NULL (true branch hit T=17 vs L T=0).

what_input_feature: Bytes that pass the HTTP header parser so that a Curl_header_store is appended to data->state.httphdrs (a non-empty list head). This requires the literal token 'HTTP/' near offset 0x24 followed by parseable name:value header lines (e.g. 'Set-Cookie', 'Connect...', CRLF terminators 0d 0a). Only then is httphdrs.head non-NULL and the for-loop true side taken.

what_input_feature: A sfnt header whose table-directory entry tags the four ASCII bytes 'COLR' (offsets 0x0c-0x0f) AND whose COLR table parses to version == 1, so the equality test version != 1 evaluates false and execution falls through into the COLRv1 extents/paint path (lines 1993-2024).

what_input_feature: An OpenType sfnt blob whose leading bytes match the expected sfnt-version magic and whose table-directory entries carry recognized 4-byte FOURCC tags (GSUB/GPOS/MATH/...), so the parsed array length is >= hb_min_size(OpenTypeFontFile) and the gate at hb-array.hh:252 takes its false branch (returning a real reinterpret_cast<const T*> rather than &Null(T)).

what_input_feature: A structurally valid sfnt/font header whose parsed table directory yields a multi-page bit-set: pages.length must be non-zero (or count!=1) so that the small-set guard at line 92 fails and the false branch is taken. This requires byte-exact sfnt magic (00 01 00 00), a plausible numTables/offset record, and recognizable table tags (cmap, GSUN/GSUB-like) at fixed offsets that survive harfbuzz's structural sanity checks deep enough to build a second bit-set page.

These pinned multi-byte literals let parsing proceed far enough that the bit-set acquires more than one page, so resize is called with pages.length!=0 and the false branch (resolved 17134 hits) is taken.

These inputs never build a second page, so resize sees pages.length==0 && count==1, takes the true branch, and the false side stays blocked (loser hitcount 0).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which copies the operands of intercepted comparisons (the sfnt magic compare, the 4-byte table-tag fourcc compares) directly into the input at the offset the operand was loaded from, byte-exactly placing 00 01 00 00 / 'cmap' / 'GSUN'. value_profile has only the CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient and havoc/token mutators (no I2SRandReplace), so it cannot place the exact multi-byte tags and stalls before a second page is built.

what_input_feature: A well-formed OpenType/sfnt container: 4-byte sfnt version 00 01 00 00 at offset 0, a non-trivial numTables, and recognizable 4-byte table tags (e.g. 'cmap', 'GSU.', tag bytes at 0x1b-0x1e = 53 55 4e = 'SUN') that drive glyph-set population across several distinct majors, so that page_map is non-empty and bfind (the binary-search lookup at line 912) returns false (no match yet) on a fresh major, forcing the insert/store-closest path.

why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (13-57 bytes) and lack the sfnt prefix and table-tag structure; page_for fires only 20 times (L) and page_map_t::cmp 0 times (L), so the bit-set is essentially empty/never bfind-searched and the false (store-closest) side at line 912 is never taken.

This lets vpc reconstruct the exact magic/tag bytes that build a multi-major page_map; plain value_profile has only the CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient and no operand-substitution mutator, so it cannot place the exact tag bytes and stays blocked.

what_input_feature: A well-formed TrueType/OpenType sfnt header (magic 00 01 00 00 at offset 0x00) followed by recognized 4-byte table tags (e.g. 'MATH'/'GPOS' at offset 0x0c) so the font parses far enough that hb_script_get_horizontal_direction is reached with a script that falls into the contiguous RTL case block (line 589-654) containing case HB_SCRIPT_OLD_TURKIC at line 615.

what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt header: the 4-byte sfnt version magic 00 01 00 00 at offset 0x00 plus recognized 4-byte table tags (e.g. 'GPOS') so the blob parses into a real hb_font_t whose font->destroy callback is null, taking the false branch at hb-font.cc:1973.

what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes (offset 0x00-0x03) must equal a recognized non-collection SFNT signature tag such as Typ1Tag ('typ1' = 74 79 70 31), so that the switch on u.tag in get_face takes the case Typ1Tag/TrueTypeTag arm instead of falling to default.
